title: First, they took my money - Then locked my accounts
review:
This is the worst company I have ever had to deal with by a long shot!

==================================================
SHORT STORY:

Bought 2 games on 2 of my Steam accounts.
Then Steam restricted both my accounts, rendering them basically useless.
Steam fails to provide me with a valid reason WHY they did this.
All they say is that they want me to provide my 25 year old physcal copy of my Half-Life 1 key from 1998.
I didn't keep the original CD + key anymore after registering it with Steam in 2003, as both CD and key were rendered useless by registering it on Steam.

Now my money is gone.
I have 2 useless Steam accounts.

Steam stole both my money and account.
They completely refuse to coorporate with me in this issue.

==================================================
LONG STORY:

Last week my wife and I decided to buy a new game to play together. I bought the game 2 times on both my accounts.

Perfect - I thought!

One day later I open Steam and get an alert message:

----------------------------------------
> This account may have been accessed by someone else.
> Steam Support suspects your account may have been accessed by someone else. We are temporarily restricting modifications to this account as a security precaution. Please contact Steam Support so we can assist you.
>
> This account will have restricted functionality until the owner has regained access.
> Purchasing, gifting, trading, cd-key activation, playing online on VAC enabled game servers, and Steam Community access have been disabled.
----------------------------------------

That this happened on BOTH my accounts!

I quickly contacted the Steam support and got the following reply:

----------------------------------------
> In order to recover this account, we must verify your ownership of the original CD Key that was used to create this Steam account.
----------------------------------------

I am one of the oldest customers this company has. I created my Steam account in 2003 when Counter-Strike 1.6 was first made available. To register it, Half-Life 1 keys from 1998 were required.

This means:
- The CD key was from 1998 => 25 years ago.
- The CD key was registered 20 years ago with Steam

Both CD and key were then absolutely useless/worthless and not required anymore for any reason whatsoever. The key could not be registered with another Steam account and the game could only be downloaded via Steam. Why should I keep 2 useless and worthless pieces of plastic/paper for another 20 years?

Steam doesn't care at all that everything ever associated with the account (e-mail addresses, payments etc.) has my name on it. They wouldn't even accept my ID/passport as proof. I proactively suggested alternative means of proof, but they simply ignored it.

The Steam support keeps on repeating that the one and only possibility for me to have my account recovered is to provide this specific (CD key from 1998) proof.
This whole issue is absolutely absurd on so many levels and probably also illegal!
It was easier to get a copy of my birth certificate in Germany than to recover my Steam account... Just think about that...

CONCLUSION:

They take my money for the games, then they restrict my account and leave me hanging.

Great job, Steam...

P.S.:
I think that the customer support system is completely run my AI, because
- my questions are not getting answered,
- the responses I get are always the same text blocks and
- I only can give 2 answers inside the ticket until they just close it (because - you know - I failed to comply with their ultimatum about the 25 year old CD key).

I now have a buggy version of the main game which is now virtually unplayable and wasn't bugged when I played the old version two years ago. The game developers have told me they can't fix (repair) the bugs so I am left with a faulty product which breaches my rights as a consumer. There is no direct email address for Steam Support and no option to place a general complaint about their poor support. I am now faced with shelling out for recorded delivery to send a formal complaint by surface mail to Steam's head office in my country. I'm not going to buy games from them in the future!

title: Steam tries to force people to quit Win 7, do not obey if you have a backbone
review:
It used to be alright, particularly with games which use the 'workshop' feature for loading up mods.
I was once sceptical about an uniformed platform you are dependent on to play your games. Over time got used to it.
Now in april 2023 Valve (steam) announced they are going to shut off their platform to users of Windows 7 at the end of this year, so from 2024 on we cannot use any games we spent money on, in good faith. I am not bowing to Steam's demands what OS I should be using to stay in touch with my purchases.
Wait. This whole story reminds us that we don't own games here, we seem to 'rent' them until the platform finds a reason to lock them away from us if we don't obey.
They could just leave a Steam client running especially for Win7 like forever, no? Win7 is 64bit just like Win10 (just better), so the excuse is rather weak, stating some Chrome browser (which I never use) can't run any longer on Win7, so bye-bye to you all.
I advise everybody no longer to buy games on Steam and look for a DRM-free place, like GOG is. Unfortunately some games are Steam-exclusively "sold" (rented), in this case, pirated versions may be the last option - hey, you paid for them already, that is the best excuse ever to get yourself a copy which will run regardless.
After all, I was not so wrong when I had my reservations about Steam back in the days...

title: Steaming pile of ####
review:
Steam? More like a steaming pile of excrement!
I bought Doom Eternal on sale a while back (or at least that's what I thought), I finally got around to playing it and the main campaign is missing. I asked for a refund, explaining that the game appeared broken, but was denied this because its been more than two weeks since my purchase, usually Id say fair enough, its in their terms of service right? But when the game doesn't work? That's not cool. I had to go back and forth with Bethesda's customer service that finally told me what I had begun to suspected, I had in fact bought a bundle that didn't contain the base game, fair enough. I will just buy that so I can play then, but no. the steam store was adamant that I already owned the product after placing it in the basket and being told I cant buy it, I removed it tried again, over an over again and over again, until something must have snapped and I was finally able to complete my purchase. And make the game work.
What pisses me of is that instead of Steams support actually supporting the games they sell and simply telling me: Oh, You need to buy the base game. And that would be the end of it, they offer NO support! They refuse my refund request on the arbitrary grounds that it more than two weeks since I made my purchase even though I hadn't played the game and the game appeared to be broken. When they could just as easily helped me resolve the issue. But no. this is how they treat loyal customers, they cant even take the time to look at the issue and tell me that I'm missing the base game which I did gladly buy, but when I explain the situation and ask for help or a measly 10 dollar refund, all I get is a big F you!
Sadly unclear marketing, and non existing customer support and quality control of the games in the steam store is the new standard for Steam.
Maybe someday a company that actually values their customer relationships will come along and make Steam obsolete.
One can only hope.

title: Valve and Steam unfair ban
review:
HT-879W-5J42-2VRP
I am writing to address an unfortunate situation regarding the recent suspension of my account by Valve employee KAL. The reason provided for the suspension was an alleged attempt to gift items from my alternative account to my main account. However, I believe this action was unjustified and I kindly request a re-evaluation of the situation.

I encountered an issue with gifting items and promptly opened a ticket to seek assistance. To my surprise, my account was banned shortly after by KAL from Valve. According to the ban notice, it was stated that my account was being used for commercial purposes, but I assure you that there is no evidence to support such a claim.

I deeply value my experience as a member of the Steam community, and I have always adhered to the platform's terms of service and guidelines. I have never engaged in any activities that violate the terms or seek to exploit the system for personal gain. The intention behind gifting items from my alternative account to my main account was purely for personal convenience and not for any commercial purposes.

I kindly request that you review the details of this case and reconsider the suspension of my account. I am confident that a thorough examination of the facts will demonstrate my commitment to maintaining a positive and fair gaming environment on Steam. I would greatly appreciate your support and prompt resolution of this matter.

Please let me know if there are any additional steps or information required from my end to facilitate this re-evaluation process. I look forward to your response and the opportunity to continue enjoying the Steam platform without any unwarranted restrictions.

Thank you for your attention and assistance.

title: steam cheated me
review:
Hello,

My Steam account, which had about $2000 worth of cash and items, has been permanently restricted for the following reason, and I am unable to access my funds:
"This account has been flagged by Steam Support for violating the terms of the Steam Subscriber Agreement. Buying games, sending gifts, trading, selling on the Community Market, and activating product keys have been invalidated."
I have asked multiple times for clarification on what exactly I have violated, but they are not giving me a clear answer. They keep sending the same message, which I believe is an automated response:
"The account in question has been locked for violating the Steam Subscriber Agreement. Based on trading, community reports, and other factors, it was determined that the account was being used for commercial purposes." What does it mean to be used for commercial purposes? I did not buy my account from anyone, nor have I sold it to anyone. It is my own account. I did not cheat in any way. I have an active game that I purchased with money on Steam. Like thousands of other Steam users, I also trade on the marketplace. How can Steam, which people trust and entrust with their money, be so unreliable? I want to know exactly why my account has been seized.

Thank you.

title: Steam wallet and card awareness
review:
Steam wallet and card awareness

I got scammed for all my money in steam marketplace because their "all sales are final" rule.
I did the whole account security recommendations and ran the anti malware.
I think that the fact steam does not take any action towards what clearly looks suspicious, im not the first person that has been scammed that way and probably wont be the last, especially since steam doesn't do anything to help the victims, those who keep using this system of buying items through community market will continue to do so as they can do it without repercusions and all steam will do it's pat their backs.
No one would buy items worth cents for all their money, ever, even if you're donating to somebody you do it via gifts, not giving away all the money, steam should clearly look at this issue and stop protecting the obvious theft. It's such a dissapointment that this type of thing is overlooked and often ignored.
What is the point of reporting suspicious activity if nothing will be done to protect those who have been affected.

To many people keep getting this issue, and steam just doesn't do anything, they should implement a system where if an item is purchased for more than 100 times the cost then something is going on and put the sale on hold until resolved. Even holding the purchase for 2 days to let users report the issue should be enough and would make scammers being unable to steal money so blatantly. If amazon and other sites do it while the purchase is being made and reviewed, why can't they do it on their marketplace.

I suggest you never leave money in your steam wallet or your card registered, your security is up to you, and you can report issues, but they wont help you in that.

You can get an amazing steal when a game goes on sale; I remember picking up the original Assassin's Creed for $7 or so a few months after it was released. I've had to change my password several times over the years because of me updating passwords for other things and getting everything mixed up but even that was never an issue, always able to quickly regain access to my account. If there are some performance issues for any games I purchase, I understand that it's an issue with porting, older games working on newer operating systems, and poor optimization with Steam's platform but so far I've only had such issues with Bayonetta and Fallout 3. Haven't tried playing those in a while but I heard there have been updates so fingers crossed. Overall though, have played countless hours and will play countless more (just got Red Dead Redemption 2 Ultimate Edition on sale).

title: Steam: Taking the lead from Revenge Porn Sites
review:
In the last few days, Amazon Gaming Services falsely banned tens of thousands of accounts for being bots, causing them to be flagged on Steam as having a ban on their account. Most of the banned accounts were simply inactive: people who had tried out a free to play game called "Lost Ark", played a few hours and then lost interest.

Most of those banned, including myself, don't care about not being able to play Lost Ark. The problem is that the ban is registered on their Steam Account, affecting their reputation and impacting on their ability to play other games. One example is Rust, which blocks users who have had a ban applied to their account in the last 180 days. There are also other games that check for accounts for bans for the purpose of applying trust ratings to players. When the players are rated as "un-trusted" it leads to them being excluded by other players.

While I completely support banning botters, cheats and disruptive players, I don't agree that it is ok to misrepresent over 10,000 players as cheaters, simply because they tried out a free to play game six months ago and didn't continue playing.

Steam designed, implemented and own the platform on which this is happening. Even though Amazon Gaming Services have indicated the mass banning was a mistake on their part, Steam have done absolutely nothing for the players that still are flagged as banned on Steam as a result. Steam are basically acting in the same way a revenge porn site: creating the means and the medium for publishers to post harmful and inaccurate information, but taking no responsibility and saying it's not their fault and doing nothing to remove the harmful content they have facilitated.

title: poor customer experience
review:
as you have read in other threads steam customer support seems to hold the line on if you play a game longer than 2 hours then you cannot get a refund. doesn't matter if you had it 1 day or 14, the policy states clearly if you play a game longer than 2 hours you cant get a refund, nor can you get credit. below are my issues with this trash.

customer service- its the ability to review a customers experience and make the best judgment for the better of the company and the customer. not saying Steam should refund all claims but they should at least review each one. is the customer habitual? is their request within some reason? and the number 1 rule of customer service is it right? how would you feel if you were in their shoes? .....

the answer! steam doesn't care about their millions of customers instead a bot or a person without any power reads the request vs. the policy and if you are over 2 hours of game play or 14 days past the purchase date, copy....paste the unfortunate no answer! congratulations steam you really can save a ton of money by reducing the customer service department to 0 humans.

retail me please- lets say a good amount of their customer base has the ability to shop at a Walmart, or Best buy, or Game Stop, ect. check their return policy... does it say any unreasonable statement like if you play the game more than 2 hours... no ... it doesn't. however not only will most of these places refund your money within 14 days but even if you don't have a receipt, they can provide a store credit, and you want to know how they can do this? with real people who have the power to weigh what is the best decision.

the customer is not always right- i couldn't agree more. but when you can see how many purchases and the amount of money they spend and will likely spend more, it may be best to find the grey in the black and white of your policy.

the reason i have resorted to bashing Steam and sharing my experience- why not list a customer support number?

for this sole reason i will try everything i can to avoid further purchases from steam. you do not have a monopoly, i can find other ways to play games. its just sad that something so simple as this can lose so much integrity for your company.

title: The Ultimate Gaming Destination: My Experience with Steam
review:
I have been a huge fan of Steam for years, and it continues to be my go-to store for purchasing and storing PC games. From the vast selection of titles to the seamless purchasing process and reliable downloads, I have always had a fantastic experience with Steam.

One of the things that I love most about Steam is the convenience of having all of my games in one place. I no longer have to worry about losing physical copies or tracking down installation files, as everything is easily accessible through my Steam library. Plus, with the ability to download and play games on multiple devices, I am never far from my favorite titles.

I have also had great experiences with Steam's customer support. Whenever I have had a question or issue, I have always received prompt and helpful assistance from their team. This has been particularly valuable when it comes to troubleshooting issues with specific games or resolving problems with my account.

Overall, I am extremely satisfied with my experiences with Steam and highly recommend it to anyone who loves PC gaming. The store is reliable, convenient, and offers an incredible selection of titles. Plus, with excellent customer support and multiplayer options, it truly is a one-stop shop for all things gaming.

title: Erm…I’d avoid if I were you
review:
A couple of issues:

Steam Deck Purchase
Purchased a steam deck through Steam. Informed that it would be a 1-2 week delivery. Now it’s still in the Netherlands and was dispatched on the 5th December. Nobody at Steam seems to want to help. It’s hard trying to get them to understand that if you put a lot of money down for an Xmas gift that you can’t just go out and spend even more money to replace a gift that they can’t seem to deliver. They blame strikes but that didn’t seem to stop the docking station from being delivered. They’re expecting us to wait another 6 days until we can launch an investigation and try and get a refund. Terrible service.

Download refunds:
I brought call of duty modern warfare 2 from their store recently. The game played well to begin with but then the game got updated which made the game unplayable. The problem here is that the refund policy only covers you for the first two hours of play and beyond that you can’t claim any compensation or refund if the game because unusable beyond this point. Games get updated a lot and it only takes a tweak of the game or the driver then the game because a pain to run. The download refund policy should also include updates.

With the steam deck and the downloads being a problem it not only damages confidence in using steam but also means I have to sort solutions to these problems. Hopefully because we paid through PayPal we’ll hopefully get something back. I feel sorry to PC gamers…apparently steam is the best place to get software for PC…if this is the quality of service of gamers have to experience you can understand why they get a bit shirty with console gaming.

If you Google steam deck deliveries loads seem to go missing. The refund policy is a bit pants. Such a disappointment. I also feel sorry for steam because these issues impacts people wanting to use the service and will ultimately impact the money they bring in.

title: Beware: Customer support and service is abysmal.
review: With the new QR code updates to steam guard, someone was easily able to hack into my account, blocking me out and stealing, my personal information, using my credit card and stealing thousands of dollars worth of in game items. I've been using Steam fine for at least a decade. Steam's Guards 2-step mobile verification never alerted me of anyone else logging into my account, which is supposed to happen. I felt falsely safe. Trying to communicate w/ Support to help, was almost non existent, extremely slow and very non remorseful about the situation, even making up new terms for someone hacking into my account and pretty much blaming me, the victim. I found our Steam is an unreliable and untrustworthy game client. A giant at it, I love gaming and their games for sure, but I can't trust the company anymore with my information anymore, because when I did and and something horrible happened, they support either flat out ignored my pleas for help or gave me some already prewritten script after the fact of what to say to make them seem like it wasn't their fault. You can not call nor can you email them. You're pretty much at their beckoning whim to respond, even if someone's password blocked you from your own account and is using your credit card to download skins and games for themselves for their other account. Before I uninstalled the game client, I looked up the hacker, with a clear record of it was and Steam had no punishment for it. I looked at some of the trade market items for sale and some still say my name for gifted items I tried to gift my son . I really don't know what to say, besides, invest, pay and play at this gaming client at your own risk. Waiting for days for a response as someone steals your identity, from a gaming client of millions, is pretty horrid. I've dealt with Xbox, PlayStation, Blizzard with little mishaps and have always been at least satisfied and understand the out come. They have unfortunately lost a very dedicated fan and also the family of future gamers I've been raising. I can't even image how devastating something like this would be for a child or young adult, so I'm going to spare them from that and switch to more reliable gaming clients.

title: Awful
review:
Very, very awful Steam Support and Moderation Staff.

I've been a victim of targeted bullying and antagonizing behavior committed by a string of users from within the Steam Forum itself. I've had my public game reviews bombarded and mocked multiple times. I have tried to play nice for the first couple months and have made many reports.

My reports have been ignored, brushed under the rug and every disciplinary action was taken out on me. I was banned 4 times from the steam forums. Understandably, my behavior has been unacceptable per time coming back from the bannings.

However, that doesn't devalue the fact that EVERY single user is to be beholden under the rules of the Steam Forum and Community Guidelines. Why is it, am I the only one punished when there had been insurmountable evidence that shows other users picking at, belittling, berating, harassing, stalking and provoking me.

Instead, my posts are removed and I'm painted as the problematic scapegoat in the community. Moderators and Steam Support have given me the cold shoulder, but they're right on me in seconds to issue bans and warnings.

How is any of this fair? What is it telling when your support staff do not hold a neutral judgment on anything that happens in the community? That by not dealing with the troublesome users, you are rewarding and enabling them to continue to do as they like by having them jump on people they don't like when they post topics and threads of discussion in the forums.

Moreso, allowing them to get away with instigating issues with other users who have otherwise contributed to the Steam Platform with game reviews.

Again, how is any of this fair to punish and shelve one user when multiple users have committed similar faults? It isn't and I think the whole moderation staff need to undergo sensitivity training and refine their judgmental skills or just be let go entirely to find newer people who can do a fairer job that they couldn't.

title: Steam is a terrible company
review:
Steam is a terrible company. Band of thieves would be a better description of them. Example, they refuse to give refunds for games that don't work, they sell old games that are incomplete, missing files, video, and sound. The steam client running in the background is a data hog.
The perfect example is the recent Sims 4 debacle. After the most recent Sims 4 update the game became unplayable for millions of players. (I know that is not steams fault, but I'm getting to it.) This Sims 4 update also added things to the game that myself and many other players didn't want and EA announced that these were not optional and could not be turned off. After seeing all this I went into steam and turned off updates for the Sims 4, so it didn't break my game. But now the game will not launch with out the update, but if you install the update you cant play the game. Now, back to the EVIL steam. So I now have a game that I payed over $50 dollars for, that I can not play. Will steam give me a refund. No of course they wont. All they care about is that I played the game more than 2 hours. Their customer support for refunds is a bot that checks the title of the game and play time, if it's over 2 hours, or has been more than 2 weeks. No refund. I sent no less than 40 request for a refund explaining all the different reasons that the game was suddenly made unplayable. Same auto generated reply everytime, from a non replyable email. steam is a worthless company with over priced games, and predatory practices, and on top of all that, keep in mind that when you "BUY" a game from steam, you don't own it. If you uninstall steam, none of "your" games will work, as a matter of fact if you don't think ahead and move them, uninstalling steam also uninstalls all of "your" games.
steam is EVIL, they should be shut down and have to refunded every penny they have stolen from customers. Do the world a favor and DO NOT use steam, buy a physical copy of the game. Developers that don't release physical copies will start releasing them when people stop falling for this predatory practice of "SELLING" you games that you don't own

title: Abysmal “Customer Service”
review:
Admittedly, Steam customer service is appalling. Let us look at Valve/Steam’s business model. We have an online merchant (storefront) selling and streaming games on their ‘distribution’ platform. The breadth and depth of their customer and technical support does not extend beyond referring customers to the software developer or other Steam customers (forum). Very shrewd business operation would you agree? This allows Valve/Steam to maintain low overhead while assuming no responsibility for post-sales support. In effect, Valve/Steam is a third-party company that sells software as-is and processes credit card transactions. Billing is efficient and instantaneous while refunds are equivalent to a rigged card game where the dealer always prevails. In addition, responses are canned and fail to address the specific issue.
For example, customer purchased Street Fighter V. Game did not have sound when connected to a large screen HDTV. Other games tested on Valve/Steam distribution platform functioned normally. In fact, Street Fighter IV functions as designed. Customer reached out to Steam and received a canned response to contact Capcom. Contacted Capcom and received no response. Posted the issue in the Steam forums and received no response. Frustrated, contacted Steam to request a refund and was immediately denied. Not a word regarding my technical issue, only referring to the time-usage policy. The more humorous aspect of this is the rabid responses received when posting this experience within the Steam forum.
One would suspect that the Steam protectors were dispatched in full force to stifle any critics of this glorified software vender. Not withstanding the sunk cost, it was entertaining to see the myriad responses when “charge back” was voiced. Couldn’t obtain a single response initially but the careful use of these two blasphemous words garnered immediate attention. Why? Admittedly, the hard-hitting responses were expected as a charge back is the only way the customer can retain some type of control of his/her financial transaction (investment). With a claimed customer base of 27M users, we are not dealing with Ph.D.’s. In the present case, outside of the repeated mimicking of the return policy, there was absolutely no comprehension of the issue, time wasted, and money lost.
The highlight of the forum exchange was a satirical response from a dimwit posting a link to an obscure third-party website that purported to address the glitch with SFV. Although, Valve/Steam, Capcom, and the Forum soldiers were unable/unwilling to provide any initial assistance, customers are expected to be aware of an obscure website. Shame on the customer for failing to find one website out of the 1.2B websites on the Internet!
The key takeaways from this frustrating and oddly entertaining experience are numerous. Purchase software at your own risk, software is sold ‘as is’, return policy supersedes all situations, Steam provides no technical support, technical support is the responsibility of the software developer, and the software developer has no obligation to provide technical support. In other words, do not waste your time trying to engage customer service, software developer, or the kids in the forum. Request a refund immediately and move on. Valve/Steam is NOT in the business of issuing returns and thus, do not allow yourself to be bullied. The intent of Valve/Steam is to evade the customer so that he/she writes-off their losses and moves on. Most importantly, as Kryptonite weakens Superman, the charge-back weakens Steam. Do not be afraid to use this and be prepared to engage the Steam mob if mentioning this in the forums (sandbox).
From a consumer protection perspective, it is believed that Valve/Steam does have a level of responsibility to address/resolve customer concerns. The link between Valve/Steam and the developer is intentionally ambiguous. Neither want to increase headcount and manage post-sales support as it does not provide the short-term revenue as does processing credit card transactions. This is one of the reasons why pirating of software persists. Why spend/lose $40 on SFV and deal with these headaches when it can be obtained for free? Based on the sheer volume of complaints, the consumer must leverage the charge-back process when appropriate and until Valve/Steam makes the necessary improvements. Incidentally, do not be surprised to read about a forthcoming class action lawsuit.

title: Worst support experience ever
review:
Absolute garbage support makes this company unbearable to purchase games from. I recently bought one of my favorite games remastered and got about 18 hours into the game and upon a steam update, my save files all went corrupt. I have tried 4 different methods and spent a fraction of the hours playing to try and repair the files, or research how to back them up.

Most fingers point to steams abysmal cloud storage system for their issues and have found fixes, but none of them have worked in my situation, so now I’ve spent hours trying to contact them or get a refund. They are impossible to contact, and try to point the finger at square Enix, even though it’s clearly their file storage system failing, and when I request a refund, it’s promptly refused without human review because I have played over 2 hours.

My game is unplayable at this point, because who wants to play another 18 hours over to have to potentially restart, and to even test whether it will even save, I would have to run through the whole beginning sequence and waste my time if it doesn’t.

So beyond frustrated with all my time wasted.

Edit: Square enix itself got back to me almost immediately to my complete and utter disbelief. They were quick to help, and provided a list of file locations to check and relay the information back to them so they could diagnose and fix my issue.

This unfortunately wasn’t my first rodeo with Steam, so I thanked Square Enix and let them know I would simply repurchase CC on the Switch as I’ve decided to leave steam permanently. The fact that a company who’s reputation is that large took ample time to reply multiple times and fast made it clear that I no longer wish to support steam, and will happily spend that money to support a company that actually cares.

title: Haters gonna hate
review:
You know, it didn't surprise me one bit when I saw all the negative reviews of Steam. We gamers are some passionate, opinionated people, man, and Steam is one of those big-ass companies we love to hate. But if I'm honest, I have never had a single problem with Steam since about 2006, when they refused to merge my two accounts -- ultimately their right, I suppose, but their security system back then used to be clunky af and their customer service department used to be a whole separate entity that made it really difficult to get anything resolved. They are much more normalized now. And since that one hiccup, I gotta tell ya, I've never had a problem. They refund crap games every time I ask with no hassle, they deliver gifts immediately, they have a righteous selection of big dev and indie games, and I've never had technical problems with Steam itself (sure some buggy "forever in Alpha" games crash, but that's not Steam, that's a Buyer Beware situation with the game devs).

Tl;dr, yeah Steam has become the easy target to hate, but I use them exclusively for all my gaming except Riot and Blizzard games and have literally zero complaints.

*Have spotty internet connection*
*Buy single player game*
*play for a while*
Steam a few days later: "can't connect to server. Try again, or start in offline mode?"
*Having spotty internet, chooses offline mode*
Steam: "bet. Connecting...
Connecting...
Connecting...
Connecting...
Connecting...
Connecting...
Connecting...
Connecting...
Connecting...
Connecting...
Connecting...
..."
...
An hour and a half later I'm sure you can guess what I'm being told the program is doing. This should be embarrassing, but then again if you have no shame how can you possibly be embarrassed?
I draw the line when a company prohibits me from playing the games I purchased. This is a simple fix, too, the only thing lacking is concern. Don't buy games on steam, they just lock you out of them.

title: Gone Downhill
review: I've been a loyal Steam customer for nearly 10 years now, ever since I built my first gaming computer. With the new fiasco that has been the Battlefield 2040 release I've been shown the horror that is Steam customer support. I bought the game while it was in Early Access, as shown on the Steam Store. The game was buggy, matchmaking was near impossible, and you couldn't even edit your controls. The very next day after I attempted to play, DICE "fixed" the game by removing promised features. I brought this to Steam Support's attention and was promptly told it was never in Early Access by the first rep (TrustPilot won't let me link the page showing that it was in Early Access, otherwise I would link it here). The second rep I spoke to had nothing to add, not even about the first rep lying in his response to my ticket. This game launch has opened up my eyes, because the only way to get support is through an automated system and if you have to deal with people then you may as well just give up. The company has turned into another major cash grab company and I'm unsure if I will even continue to purchase games through them. (Username: Loneshadow01)

title: 17 years as a customer for nothing..
review:
Edit: Steam reached out to me an helped me solving this issue today. Because of this I am changing from 1 star to 3. Great when a company actually listen to customers and I am very happy again.

I've been a customer with Steam for around 17 years now and I've loved it all these years. The other day however, I received a gift from the company where I work, a prepaid Master Card (50 USD). Since I live in Sweden the only currency available is EUR, but to empty the card I needed to use USD because Steam only offer 5, 10, 25, 50 and 100 EUR transfers. 50 USD is around 43 EUR and I did not want to lose any money which happened several times doing the top ups in EUR.

Anyway, when I was given the card I was on my work computer and since I work for an American company I am always on a secured VPN. This means that I got the option to change to the US region and use USD, perfect! I did it and transfered my 50 USD... but now I was locked for 3 months to the US region. Sure it suck and it is quite a punishment since I did not even buy an item/game or did any trades etc.

Then I realised that I could not even buy a game now, my cards, my PayPal .. nothing was accepted because those are linked to Sweden. So pretty much I cannot buy any games AND then I found out that I will not be able to get my pre ordered Steam Deck since it was ordered in the EU region and I am now stuck in US region.

The support basically told me:
"As for your Steam Deck reservation, you will not be able to complete the purchase as your account country does not match your purchase country shipping address and payment method.

I understand that's not the answer you are looking for. Unfortunately, there's nothing more we can do in this case."

This is how Steam treats their loyal customers. The worst part is that I bought the Deck for my kids and they are expecting to get it within a few weeks.

This is the worst customer experience I've ever had. 17 years as a customer for nothing..

title: It started well
review:
It started well. Got a couple of games and enjoyed playing. First i started having problems when games crashed and saves were lost/damaged. Then they started changing their software. My steam window started to look like MS DOS with blue background. Spent hours looking for a way to contact support, had to look on external webpages. Steam support claimed that i need to install external "certificates" in to my windows(???). Part of steam called "Friends Network" is not working claiming "no connection to internet" - even after i logged in through internet. Recently Steam run a hardware survey of its users, parts of my hardware were incorrectly identified. If steam software cant recognize users hardware, things wont work.
I wont buy anything else, despite still playing, as i don't know how long it will work.
(steam username: kindowaz)

title: Steam has become another money grabbing cog
review:
After years of gaming and multiple purchases, I eventually came to a point where I needed customer support. What a surprise! There is none. It's all bots or other users, but no one actually from Steam.
After spending $100's on games over the years, I came across a development tool that I could not get to work ($14). I spent 4 hours trying to get it to work and steam refused my refund because it was used for over 2 hours....It's not even a game.
Now try to speak to someone to challenge this decision. Nope, just bots that are pre-programmed to decline your request.
You want to go the Steam route....okay, just remember the moment it goes off the rails, there is no one at Steam interested in helping you.
I'm leaving Steam....very disappointed.

I have spent at least $200 - $400 on steam and this is how I get repaid. if you read this steam, my email is Sonicsk278@gmailcom. I'd like my money back, but I don't expect to get any back. I have since switched to epic games... and it looks and feels alot better. Thanks for reading my review.

title: Do Not Use Steam Wish List for Gifts

Steam gave no consideration to the situation resulting in the double purchase. Steam does not flag or indicate that an item from someone's Wish List has already been purchased. So, your gift purchase may be a duplicate without your knowledge. I'm not sure what will happen Christmas morning when my friend gets 2 copies of the game.

title: After being a Steam user for about 10 years
review:
After being a Steam user for about 10 years. Steam has lost this customer.

First on a purchase of a game they wouldnt help resolve technical issues and then refused to provide a refund claiming I had played the game, which is false because I never got the game to work.

Second, I purchased a game and the next day they started a summer sale, I asked for a refund of the difference that day, they did not refund and under California consumer law their is a right to a refund for 7 days from date of purchase. I had to do a partial chargeback for only the difference of what I paid and the discounted price from the sale. Steam then restricted my access so I cannot play the game, and they want me to pay full price to unrestrict it, therefore they have committed theft of what I have spent on the game of $35.99 an they want $59.99 more. They also restricted me from purchasing new games.

I am considering a chargeback on the remaining $35.99 but if steam does not lift the account restrictions entirely I have no choice but to never use them again. I will also consider legal action. It makes things worse that there is no phone number to call to try to resolve issues over the phone.

I have over 300 games on my steam account, I have over $3000 invested into it. This is unacceptable and their customer serve is worthless. Especially the employee called "May"

title: steam breach of data protection laws
review: i have a strong warning for anyone who uses or wishes to use this platform.. the email account i have linked to my steam account was flagged in a recent data breach in which my login and password had been leaked.. i lost all access to this email account, i tried to log into steam to change my email and login details and to check i had removed my payment method after my last purchase from the platform. i could not login as steam guard sent the required code to the the email address that had its login details leaked thus preventing me from logging in to the platform and giving anybody who purchased my email login details all they needed to gain access to my steam account. i contacted steam to make them aware of what was going on and they decided securing my account and payment details was not of any priority. it took over 10 hours for then to resolve the issue, would have been longer if i did not threaten then with the data protection act and legal action. they did not lock the account to prevent any login attemps using my login in details.no concern was show that my personal data was at risk. thankfully i asked my bank to put a temp block on my debit card until steam sorted this out. if you use this platform DO NOT store your payment details on the site, i would advise that you remove them after any purchases you make. every other online account or platform i use were able to confirm that i was whom i was claiming to be and allowed me to change my email and login details within minutes no problems at all. but steam it took over 10 hours to secure my account and data.their security in this case was as bigger threat as the data breach that leaked my info.this company has show that they do not care about the security of your data stored on your account on their platform. if you must use this platform for god sake remember do not store your payment details on the platform and do not expect them to protect you if you fall victim to a data breach and do not expect them to act quickly when you inform them that your account and data is are risk. i will be lodging formal complaints to steam and have passed on all emails sent to and received from steam to a lawyer who will be making a complaint for breaching the data protection act with regards to steam preventing me from safe guarding my personal data stored on their platform and a complaint against steam for failing to protect my personal data stored on their platform.

title: Scam by Steam or Terrible Business Practice
review: I received an e-mail saying that I had purchased two Steam Games. I answered the e-mail and then looked at my Steam account where I found two games having been purchased for $55.00. I notified Steam that I did not purchase these games. Steam said they found the hacker and charged him the $55.00 but that they cannot remove the fraudulent charges from my account. They said I must pay $55.00 in order to access my account, download games, etc. I have purchased dozens of games over the years from Steam and Steam has basically blocked me from playing any of these games that I have purchased unless I pay the $55.00 for items ordered by a "Hacker". Steam says it cannot remove fraudulent charges from my account. So, I'm to believe a 360 Billion Dollar company cannot remove a $55.00 fraudulent charge from one of their customer's account. Sounds to me like Steam is the "Hacker".

title: Steam is happy to let game studios scam…
review:
Steam is happy to let game studios scam users.
I purchased Back 4 Blood a couple of days before game launch (pre-order), when the game launched I was unable to play it because of an in-game error message accusing me of cheats. Of course, not actually cheating, this issue was soon reported to the game studio and then has been promptly ignored for over a month. Not just me with this issue, many many others.

Unhappy with this outcome of not being able to play something I paid $150 for, I contacted Steam support twice, asking for a refund, as per Australian consumer law, a product with a major defect should be fixed or refunded, to which Steam and B4B game maker denied.
Unhappy with this outcome also, I contacted Steam support again, which was unsuccessful. They even decided to create a ticket for me, after seeing how annoyed I was at being scammed, but then after a couple more days they decided there's still nothing they are willing to do and closed the ticket.

If you want to be scammed, go ahead and use Steam / buy TRS games. I will be opting out of this and using other game stores instead. I highly recommend any other Australians that have had similar issues contact the ACCC and let them know, as I have done. Hopefully they can hold Valve to account again in the future.

review: This platform has lost its way. Their mistakes is your loss. A money pit you will lose at. Played on this platform for 6 years, spent a lot of money and have left in disgust. title: Untrustworthy service

Two big issues here. First, a publisher can at any time and for any reason remove your game from your library. Already had it happen a few times, with steam support essentially saying "not our problem." At which point you have to start fighting with the publisher to get your purchase back.
Second, steam allows blatant harassment in the forums. Someone decided that they were going to create a long post in a game forum dedicated to attacking a particular user (me) in order to start a witch hunt, and Valve refused to do anything about it. Not only was it inappropriate in a game forum, it was also just a really scummy thing to do.
Seriously, Valve needs to get off their butts and properly address these issues.

title: Steam Support, and in general, is GARBAGE
review:
Hello all,

I am posting this again with some changes, I received two different flags on my account and this is the review for the other flagging.

I recently made another steam account, I have other steam accounts that have games on them and one of those games is Counter Strike Global Offensive, I got on CS and intended to purchase some new skins. I went to use the money and got flagged for fraudulent activity.

I am glad that there are safety protocols for this, however; I contacted steam support and haven't received any responses at all! needless to say I was irritated about it. I have contacted them multiple times and to no avail. The account has the same email address linked, I am on the same I.P., and I am using the same paypal account. All of this would clearly indicate that this is not fraudulent, yet here we are. I also requested a refund, and have not received it. At this point, I am questioning if there is anyone that actually works in steam support or not.

I am writing this to make people aware that Steam is not the company that they used to be. They do not care about their customers as much as they used to and do not give them the time of day or respect they deserve. I hope that this changes in the future as I do remember the times when Steam would take care of their customers and help them right away. Until then I think that I am done dealing with Steam all together.

Hope that this review is seen by as many people as possible so they don't have to deal with the bs that I am.

review:
Customer support is basically run by bots.
I bought New World, proceeded to spend 5 hours in Queues just to play 90 mins. After that time, it was quite clear this game wasn't for me and I asked for a refund.
I explained that in my ticket, yet just received a generic response that my playtime was over 2 hours therefore no refund.
After finding a different way to request a refund (hoping to at least obtain an actual response), I was once again met with a generic response, +2 hours no refund.
Just to make this clear: my problem is not about being denied the refund itself (although it seems clear that I should, as there is no way to actually play the game without waiting 4+ hours queue).
The real problem, is that I'm still not sure a single person even read one of my requests, as all the responses I received were copy-pasted automatic responses.
This is not customer service, it is automated dismissal.

title: Refund experience
review:
Purchased a game to play a game mode. Found out after 6+ hours of playtime (I left the app open while doing other things) that the game mode had bugs that made it unplayable.

I went through the automated refund request system and was denied 4 times.

However, when I selected the option to 'ask a question,' I was able to get my refund request reviewed by a Steam technician.

They understood the situation and were able to refund the purchase to 'steam wallet' (store credit), which is an outcome I can be happy with.

Downsides: Refund requests automatically declined with >2 hours play time due to policy.

Upsides: There is some wiggle room, especially if you have not abused the refund system.

This seems like a happy medium to me. A company won't be trolled by unreasonable customers, and a customer can get a refund for a defective product.

I'm giving this 4 stars because other support teams for other companies have been far worse (looking at you Square Enix). Steam at least has a way to get help while only having to jump through one hoop rather than many hoops.

They basically said that I needed to get a gift card for world of warships if that’s what he wanted. For example- downloading updates to games I have used for years now needing a 'third party' screen to play. This is happening more and more to the games and then causes them to crash or simply not load. Customer Services was brilliant but now has become poor. Limited refund policy so you download a game and because it uses the publishers 'launcher' Steam don't support but ask you to contact the company directly which is quite poor. Sort it out and get back to what you're good at- providing easy access to great games without all this 'launcher' crap!

title: Selling broken games with 2 hrs return policy.
review: They sold me a game that worked for about 13 hrs (7% of the total game roughly) and then stopped working, crashing at launch. I re-installed it, verified the files, moved folders, everything. First of all the game should have worked effortlessly and not break in the first place like any product, but anyway I have been patient, these things happen sometimes. I tried to get support but they say "You need to contact the third-party manufacturer for support", which translated means: "we don't want to help our customers and just wanna give the problem to someone else even though we took your money". I did so, no reply. I asked Steam for a refund since they took the money in exchange for a broken product (which is borderline illegal), to which they replied "because you played it for more than 2 hrs we won't refund it". So as long as your game lasts that long they don't care if it stops running after or if their platform interferes with its launch and even if it's a small problem they won't help as there is no support from them willingly. So be aware, when you buy a game you're actually buying just 2 hrs of gameplay, everything else after it's at your own risk, and no support will be given under any circumstance. Wow...just wow...I feel bad for whoever works at Steam and has to steal money from regular people on behalf of their corporation. Don't come and cry when people crack games not to deal with this and your company goes under.

The information I provided was NOT false, in fact it can be backed up with genuine science. Also Dr. Byram Bridle is NOT some random "conspiracy theorist", he is a fully qualified viral immunologist from Canada. So for Steam to claim it was false information, well that's just ridiculous and ignorant and it clearly shows that they (along with the other social media platforms) are following the New World Order agenda.

I have contacted Steam Support, however it seems like they are not interested in helping me. All they seem to do is palm me off with excuses and lies to justify the ban and to keep me quiet. I've decided to put my foot down and not accept these blatant lies that they keep churning out, but it doesn't seem to be getting me anywhere. As the saying goes. People who dare to tell the truth always get the most flak...

title: I'm not really a fan of steam, but
review:
I'm not a fan of the steam-flavored monopoly, but I haven't had problems with crashing like others have. As a user interface designer, I find their layout/color options are abysmal, (I can *never* see the search box on my monitor, for example), and like others I find their speed to update the interface to be ridiculously slow (but a little bit faster/better lately)

Also, there are errors that have been around for years and haven't gotten fixed.

I have sent in a few ideas with "please don't reply to me, just forward this" and one of them got implemented not much later.

I've sent in a few help requests and gotten mostly canned answers, but with a little work, the issues were resolved satisfactorily. (Mostly comes from me not being able to use their interface)... I almost never have this sort of issue in any other game or game related software.

Once, many, many years ago, I had created an account, forgot about it, and then used their little account finder tool to see if I had an account or not. The account finder tool told me my account name didn't exist (when it actually did), so I assumed I must not have made the account. When I found my account by talking to a friend who had it on their friends list, I tried to contact support. But the staff gave me the run around until I told them to use the finder tool with my username. The "fix" is I now have 2 steam accounts, one with just a few games on it. Oh well. I'd like to think they would have come up with better ways to verify user accounts now and let you transfer data, google allows for this. But I doubt it.

I have had a few games that I got that don't work anymore (not because of steam), but I blame the game company for that, not steam. I leave a bad review on the game and move on.

I haven't tried to return a game I just bought, so I don't have the ability to comment on that.

The games are great, the platform is great, customer service is 'OK' at best, their business practices on the other hand... absolutely despicable.

title: Can't run Grand Theft Auto V on Linux Mint anymore!
review: When the pandemic was tough, I got bored and was recommended Steam. I bought a few games like Tennis Elbow, King's Quest and a pinball game. Everything was fine for months. Until one day Grand Theft Auto didn't work! It was just a disaster! I tried different versions of Proton (a special program that let's you play Windows games on Linux), I tried to change the settings. It was like this for a couple of weeks. I ask for help and told them what was wrong. They said keep up-to-date with Github and they consistently look at bugs with games. I asked them for a refund, they said no. I asked them for earlier versions of Proton and the Rockstar application engine that is also used to run the game. They wouldn't do it. I looked at the options and there is no way you can turn updates off! I had it for months, and now I've had to say goodbye to it! I am extremely lucky to complete the game, but I miss it. You always want to go back to a game like this because it's so cool and realistic. They deleted all my emails I sent them via the Steam program. Completely memory holed the conversation! They are so arrogant! I have to give 2 stars because it was good for a while and it's fairly easy to tinker with Proton when it works and it worked beautiful on Linux. But it's like me buying a house and then someone says "You can't have it anymore." It's absurd like my situation and other Linux users. It doesn't matter what version of Linux you use or what kernel of Linux you are running, there are still problems. I'm screwed! It's terrible to treat people like that! I spent hundreds of dollars buying games and a couple of control pads! I just feel it's just awful! Be careful guys!!!

title: Mostly fantastic
review:
I've been on Steam for about ten and a half years now and have hardly had a problem. The software has always worked fine for me, it is very easy to install and uninstall games and the store has never given me any issues.

At first I was apprehensive due to having less ownership of the game than if I owned a physical copy. But when Civilization 5 required Steam I decided to give it a go.

Now I cannot imagine having to go back to DVD copies of games, the hassle of finding the DVD I need when I want to play and manual patching.

Although there are a few things I'd change about Steam which I'll get to below, something far worse could have taken Steam's place in terms of DRM. Steam can be launched in offline mode and games played offline. The developers can choose to allow games bought on Steam to be launched without the Steam client being open.

As well as partly being DRM, Steam has a lot of features which make my life as a gamer a lot easier. Most games support Steam cloud, backing up save files so you don't lose them if you forget to back up when changing computer. Game files can be backed up to an external hard drive to make restoring them a lot quicker than redownloading them. Compared to most competitors such as Origin, Epic Games and Ubisoft Connect, Steam has the most features and the least annoying DRM.

There are a couple of things I would change about Steam. I'd prefer it to be totally DRM free like GOG. Although it is great that indie developers are given a chance, Steam make it a bit too easy to publish and there are lots of very low quality games to sift through. You're forced to always be updated to the latest version of a game. I'd prefer if I could pause updates or easily roll back if an update gives me an issue or is not compatible with my save file.

I notice a few reviews here saying that Steam is expensive. It is if you buy most of your games full price from the Store. I recommend getting the majority of your games through Humble Bundle and other bundle sites like Fanatical and Indie Gala as well as in Steam sales. The Christmas and Summer sales are best. Getting the bulk of your games through bundles and in sales actually makes Steam an extremely cost effective way to build a library of games.

title: PURCHASE ANYTHING FROM STEAM AT YOUR OWN PERIL
review:
GETS 1 STAR AS ZERO ISNT POSSIBLE

BE WARNED - STEAM's return/refund policy is basically like buying a movie, getting it home, watching it but it cuts out halfway through, and when you try to take it back for an exchange you're told "You should have known it was defective. You watched the first half so you don't qualify for any compensation".

Erm - Are you serious?
Sadly yes I am.

I purchased Final Fantasy VIII remastered, a game that takes a minimum of 30 - 35 hours to complete even just for the trophy list, but most people will take 100+ hours as its massively open world.
My purchase repeatedly crashed at the Fisherman's Harbour location after 90 hours play (got to fight off the Lockdown Blues somehow). It kept crashing even after dozens of tries at fixes with various settings (including running as admin), and always at exactly the same place. Eventually I gave up.

I contacted steam, requesting a credit (not refund) against another purchase as their game is not playable - AND WAS REFUSED?!?
Why? Because I had played the game for more than 2 hours. 2 HOURS DOESNT GET YOU PAST THE FIRST MISSION YOU ID**TS!!!!!!!!

So if any game you purchase crashes after 2 hours, its apparently not their fault, its yours for not playing the game at light speed to get to the Exception notice/glitch/Crash that might be dozens of hours into a game.
Oh yes - and if it is a Steam third party client as with Final Fantasy creators Square Enix, that too is not Steams fault, even though you downloaded the game from Steam. So basically STEAM takes the money, but any issues are someone else's problem.

I don't care about the money (it's £7.99). It's about good customer service, holding your hands up and taking responsibility for a product you sell, and for treating your customers with a modicum of common decency.
Concepts totally alien to the soulless robots working at STEAM.
My advice is that if a game crashes before the 2 hour cut off, DO NOT PLAY FURTHER. Contact STEAM immediately.

And if its after 2 hours? Just give up - they don't care and will only send you an automatic response that says:-
"We are unable to refund this purchase to your Steam Wallet at this time. Your playtime of an included product exceeds 2 hours (our refund policy maximum)."
Says it all really.

Rant over, SoapBox away.

But the ting is there are a lot of scammers on steam that try to steal your account and take your valuable items that you own on your steam account. title: WATCH OUT. Don't use steam or you may lose your games/money.

Although I have access to the original e-mail account and could tell them every game I played and roughly how many hours, they wanted more info (like the cd-key from Half-Life Orange Box which is 13+ years old) and refused to give me access to my account and my games. I literally paid money for games and now they won't let me play them. I feel robbed and I feel like they really don't care about their customers. If you need games, EPIC and ORIGIN are straight-up companies that won't rob you with red tape. I remember a time when Gabe wanted to take gaming to the next level. Now I think they're more about money than gaming experience or customer satisfaction.

title: They just break the law, and its that simple.
review: It is amazing how some large American companies just choose to flout the consumer law of other countries over and over again and by making themselves unavailable for comment they get away with it every time. I bought a faulty product, they wouldn't help me fix it despite the law stating that as the vendor it is their responsibility to do so. Then upon trying to get help from the game development team or anywhere else I could find, my trial and error took the game over the "2 hours refund policy deadline". A policy doesn't mean anything if it contradicts law. They can have as many policies as they like but if they sell you a faulty product and (in my case at lease) you can prove a faulty product has been sold, then they should refund, replace or repair. Id recommend that ANYONE that has these kinds of issues contacts the relevant ombudsman no matter how small the amount of money lost is, because these companies will continue to behave this way unless challenged. It is a matter of principle and these companies need to understand they are not above the same laws that govern homegrown companies.

I have spent thousands in my 20 years on steam, and the deny a refund on a game I can't even play?

*Update*

I got a hold of Steam support, they made it right! The rep was super nice and quite helpful. She explained that the refund request selection was not seen by a person, but was handled automatically within the set parameters. So, always reach out to them if you are slightly outside of the return threshold ( 2 hours in game, or 1 week of ownership ).

title: I have a long history of hating Steam
review:
I have a long history of hating Steam. I started to hate them when I bought a cd game that in the fine print mentioned something about needing online verification to activate on steam...I didn't think much of it and figured it was just like registering the game. So I buy the game and then go home to play. Only to have to first dl Steam, which I didn't want to have, and then the CD was basically useless. Eventually I went ahead and made an account and played my game. Years go by and I want to play my old game but obviously I have a new computer since it's been about 15 years, and so I get the old cd out and now since I can't remember what account I set up from back then the code is useless because it's a one time use code and they offer no kind of work around or customer support to get my game so I paid good money for a chore, a useless product and to borrow a game for awhile. This kind of business ethics is extremely vexing.

My kind built the gaming industry, all the good elements of it, and now we got to deal with this day in age where slow minds that play crappy app games taught the gaming industry that all they need to do is create a shady and or crappy system to extort money from people. They don't uphold their end of providing a product, they provide a gamble, you pay and the house always wins. I hope companies like this one day go extinct!

title: They do not respect their refund policy, shame on you, Steam
review:
Steam you automatically renewed my Dota 2 Plus plan, and I tried to cancel it minutes after that yesterday. You are refusing to refund that, however, it is against your own policy:

"RENEWABLE SUBSCRIPTIONS
For some content and services, Steam offers periodic (e.g. monthly, yearly) access that you pay for on a recurring basis. If a renewable subscription has not been used during the current billing cycle, you may request a refund within 48 hours of the initial purchase or within 48 hours of any automatic renewal. Content is considered used if any games within the subscription have been played during the current billing cycle or if any benefits or discounts included with the subscription have been used, consumed, modified or transferred."

title: Amazing service and very knowledgeable
review:
Once upon a time I bought a gift card for steam ($100), when I went to foolishly peel the sticker covering the code (by the way NEVER do this, always scratch the thing like a lotto ticket) and I ripped the code!

alas struck with grief I went to steam support, thinking I had effectively just shoved $100 in a blender, but I had forgot I was being helped by *Ta Daaa* STEAM SUPPORT! As I slept and dreamt horrors of never being able to own Cyberpunk2077 due to my own foolishness, the steam support crew worked tirelessly to isolate my problem and resolve my query.

I awoke to a well written and polite inquiry from them as to whether or not I had the receipt from my purchase remaining, After a cursory search, tragedy struck! I had misplaced my receipt! After several hours upturning every possible location and sifting through garbage cans, I had no choice but to conclude my quarry was lost.

This is the point when most support services would declare yours a helpless case, resigning you to endless transfers and "we're working on it, we'll get back to you" emails. But not steam support! oh no! they ramped it up a notch, assigning only the best of the best to my conundrum (Anne).

And so just as I was proceeding to analyze the ripped off sticker under a microscope to possibly glean some sort of clue, I got THE message (and just a few hours after I had informed them of the missing receipt to boot), stating that the issue was resolved!

With only the scantest of information the steam support crew had managed to track down my missing $100 and reunite me with my once-lost funds, clearly proving their absolute dominance over all competitors.

5/5 service 10/10 service 100/100 service there is not a number high enough to express my gratitude!

TL:DR In just over a day they not only managed to identify the problem but also rectify it. I am now reunited with my once lost steam funds and am off to become the proud owner of Cyperbunk2077! 5/5 service! Scratch your gift cards like a lotto ticket, never peel them & always keep your receipts.

title: Steam is going downhill
review:
Steam has for many years been useful for three reasons: keeping your library in one place, downloading and installing your games, and sales.

Client changes in 2019 through 2020 have rendered the library more difficult and frustrating to use than just browsing with Windows Explorer.

Download speeds have gotten progressively worse, month by month, since around the beginning of 2019. At this point, downloading a game requires first spending hours repeatedly trying combinations of server and VPN location just to hit 1Mbps.

Sales are still regular and good, but what use is that if we can't browse the games we've bought to launch and manage them, and can't download them without sacrificing all our free time for each one?

Many people are blaming Valve's flat management structure for this, but who knows whether that's the ultimate underlying cause? Maybe they've tried some new things that haven't worked. But whatever it is, they could just restore the client and download servers to the January 2019 version and it would be the best update of the last two years.

At this point, I honestly wish I could transfer all my software licenses to a different service. I'd leave Steam altogether, were it not for thousands of dollars in games held hostage.

For the record, I have an advanced performance ASUS laptop from 2018; I thought X-Plane might be usable since it was release in 2017. I requested a refund from Steam because I was unable to use the simulator without a laggy flight experience. But instead of being treated in good faith, I was denied a refund for using the simulator for more than a very small 2 hours (which by the way I used trying to throttle the performance settings of the simulator to make it playable, all with no luck). I even wrote them a second time saying they were being inconsistent by not refunding my for X-Plane though they had refunded me for Microsoft Flight Simulator, which I had "played" for 18 hours according to Steam. I hadn't--those 18 hours were used just trying to download the files! Why refund me for 18 hours, but not 2? I don't like being taken advantage of. Especially considering it costs them absolutely nothing to refund a game purchase since they're entirely digital and there is no added inventory or logistics cost. Their lack of ethics also strikes a nerve because all I wanted with the simulator was to use something healthy to cope with my weakened mental health/increased anxiety due to COVID, and this is how I'm treated. Thanks Steam.

title: Care with refund policy

But even though there had been zero play time Steam would not refund an unplayable game as they system showed 4 hours of play time. Steam need to develop a process by which they can see play time rather than 3rd party launcher run time, otherwise the refund policy is potentially meaningless

title: Steam Support completely ignorant of their own products
review:
Steam ticket HT-CM6D-3THR-2KMG. I had no problems with a game and all it's DLC on an older box. When installing a newer box some DLC wouldn't install despite being in my Library. The failing DLC has the same support and requirements as other DLC that didn't have an issue on either box.

Steam "Support" answers:

1. That product isn't available for [the platform that didn't have any issues]. Evidently they didn't understand what those little OS icons on the Steam Store pages are for. Or, more likely given the following answers, didn't bother to look.

2. The DLC is progress locked. Evidently they missed the part where I've already played all of the game and all of the DLC. Or the part where the game refused to load a save, instead stating that the required DLC was not installed.

3. The inability of the Steam client to install content on a new box that it has already installed successfully on an old box is the fault of the content creator. Evidently they missed (again) the part where the delta between success and failure is not the game content. Or the part where their own purchase tracking indicates the content ready to install automatically but their client refuses to do so.

4. The DLC is in your Library, therefore it's installed. Evidently they don't understand the Steam provisioning architecture or the difference between a list of accessible content in the cloud and the parts of that content that have been installed by the Steam client on a Customer's local box.

5. The base product isn't available for Linux, but is only available for Windows. Evidently they're not aware of that little Steam product called "Proton", or that the base product is on Steam's own gold-level list of Proton supported games.

title: Poor Customer Service, glitchy webstie.
review:
My story:
Two years ago, I purchased a game from Steam.I played it through once.This year I decided to play it again, but couldn't find it on my new computer, so I thought I had to buy it again. I visited Steam's website and attempted to purchase this game, but got a pop up message saying I already owned the game. But when I checked my library, it wasn't showing. So I explained that it wasn't there and tried to buy it again. Once more the same message appeared saying I already owned it. So I decided to load funds in my "wallet" in case I wanted any more games.

Later I get an email saying I had purchased a new game and the funds had been taken from my wallet. I wrote to STEAM and asked how they figured they could charge me for a game I already owned? They replied that I had two hours of play time on the game so I couldn't get a refund.I replied that since I owned the game, of course there were hours of game time. They ignored me and refused to refund the money. I applied to PayPal which I had used for game purchases and they said that STEAM stated there were no duplicate purchases in their records. Is it my fault they don't keep records from two years ago? Well, I do. And I will never buy another game from STEAM. I use two other gaming companies and have never had a problem go unresolved. The very least STEAM could do is issue a credit or a refund!

title: Long time victim.
review:
I'm writing this after having used Steam for roughly 10 years, and while it's not entirely useless there are better competitors on the market now vs when I first joined.

I wanted to give 1 star as the cons of Steam is that inconvenient to me, but I'm giving them 2 because I do enjoy the community and workshops for games that support it.

So what makes it so bad in my opinion?
The horrendous customer service and how stupidly difficult it is to get any actual help, the system they use makes it hard to find answers to even basic questions but also makes it nearly impossible to intuitively send a help ticket-- which in 2020 is something you expect from even the lowest budget sites and service, let alone a giant like Steam.
Frequent errors while purchasing games, the client crashing or just processing for an eternity before it failing-- getting an error message that you've attempted to buy too many games in the last few hours (when you've had 2 unsuccessful attempts) and getting a time out. They don't specify how many games to get a time out or how long it lasts, I've heard 10 games and 1 hour-- but gotten time outs with as little as 2 and for longer than 1 hour. Them blaming it on services like PayPal when PayPal hasn't been loaded at all and no money has been withdrawn by them, etc.
No support when there is something wrong with a game or a company ripped you off, having to send evidence is expected-- but you can't actually add anything to the message so how exactly are you supposed to do that (messaging them to ask how leads nowhere naturally)?

The positives are that with so many users there are a lot of people creating things for the workshops which is nice, and there are some good sales and indie games. When it works it works pretty well too, but really that's nothing to brag about as that's what it's supposed to do.

I'd recommend finding somewhere else to buy games if you can, for us who already have a large library of games we're stuck but for new customers-- run.

review:

I think the design is poor - there's an overwhelming amount of information on screen at any one time. Ugly and exhausting. I know I'm probably not their target audience but I think that even a 'Netflix' or 'PlayStation Store' shop front style site would be more user friendly.

I would have given it 1 star but I recently got the price of an unplayabel game refunded and that process was pretty straightforward. However, it may be worth noting I once bought a game that wouldn't load the final stage or the end cut scene (Life is Strange - maybe?) and Steam wouldn't refund because I'd played more than 80%. That policy didn't make much sense. It's a bit like paying full price for a book when someone's ripped out the final chapter.

If your problem persists then it's not solvable sorry, that's reality.
