title: I have good experiences with purchases…
review:
I have good experiences with purchases and support, however, recovering Steam accounts is a painful process and the Support website doesn't make it easy once your e-mail and login get changed by a hacker (because you can't normally recover your account past that point, unless you get an e-mail from Steam that gives you an exclusive recovery link).
Moreover, it's quite easy to lose the access to your account because despite 2-factor authentication and really 3 ways you can use 2FA (text messages, e-mails or tokens), one of them can be used to disable the remaining two. Namely, tokens can disable everything, and there are NO CONFIRMATIONS on disabling security, changing the e-mail, login credentials and the password - the mobile app doesn't show what the token is being used for, unlike an e-mail or a text message, and the app doesn't ask for a confirmation. If you lose your credentials and tokens (or type them on a realistic but fake website), the support website becomes useless and there is no way to contact support directly without knowing the e-mail to them, which isn't shown anywhere, AFAIK.

title: Be very careful of the games you buy on…
review:
Be very careful of the games you buy on Steam. They have a 14 day rule in that if you buy a game and it will not work on your computer they will then just keep your money. Even if the specs on your computer match the game. Also if you play the game for more than 2 hours and it develops problems you can not solve. Steam will also keep your money. Their response is a auto message simple saying no refund. It does not matter how good a customer you are the response is still the same. I have 35 games and usually buy a number of games in the sale. There is no way to check all these games in a 14 day time frame. Recently I tried to load one of these games and it did not work. Steam response was to simple keep my money because of the 14 day rule. Game platform has been find and games on the sale have been good value. However to keep money when a product does not work for a good customer is why I have to give such a poor rating. Also
I advise only buy a game if you have time to check it out first for bugs and problems. You can do this online. If any problems show up in the first hour request a refund right away. Remember the14 day rule check your game out as soon as you buy it. I will certainly not buy a number of games at the same
time because you do not have the time to check each one out. Keep it to one game at a time.

title: Do not recommend
review:
Do not recommend.

Steam is a game platform monopoly to me personally. Which isn't a great thing as if anything goes wrong steam can just destroy your account and all your games are tied to that one account. You can easily lose $100s if not $1,000s of dollars when it comes to a steam account alone. Figure it's usually best to diversify so I decided not to invest any more time or money into one steam account.

I was coming up on 9 years with steam and was encountering too many problems and had to just let go at that point when it came to steam (I left steam or the steam platform at that point). I should say also that I'm kind of out of gaming for now though to as well so it was more or so a neutral decision as well. The fact that it's all tied to one account though is why I can't ever waste my time on steam again as a gamer. Had a problem with 1 game in my 9 years or so and never had any vac bans myself, but it's a complete waste of time to keep my account. The problem is I don't want to risk money in something when something could happen in the long run (close call with 1 game alone). Cut my losses now since again it's a platform monopoly to me personally (not easy to diversify - may not be a legal platform monopoly though - can't go anywhere else though) to me even if that's just personally.

title: illegal policy uk based
review:
Have bought over 30 games from steam and 85% are fine and work. However when a game has a software issue its very hard to get a refund. Ive had 4/5 that have not worked either from start or mid way through games and i know 100% its a software issue. when contacting steam you only get a generic response either you have played more than 2 hours or bought it over 2 weeks ago. However as of 2015 i should have 30 days to request a repair or refund which ive offered them to repair the software and only got the same generic response even after asking several time to confirm my complaints been read. YOU CAN VIEW UK DIGITAL GOODS LAW ON THE GOV UK WEBSITE UNFORTUNATELY IM UNABLE TO POST THE LINK HERE
I have been to the ombudsman who have agreed that they are in breech of this but valve is one of the companies who refuse to cooperate with them leaving me in a difficult situation.
I have decided as f today to go through the small claims court as im in the lucky position i can afford a solicitor. We are hopeful to set a precedent so others will not have a similar issue as i have read many times the have.
Just to note valve have recently lost a big case similar to this in australia and lost a case in france in relation to re selling games.
Its time we stood up to large companies bullying customers that cant afford to have their rights respected.
Im aware im only fighting for £40 - £50 but its principle. If no one complaines they will keep doing it to customers

review:
Wow. It's still unclaimed. No surprise there. Clearly they don't want to read the feedback.

I feel Valve who own Steam really don't care and here is why:

• They didn't seem to care about their new competition at Epic Games.
• They didn't seem to do anything competitive with their pricing and what the cut they take from developers.
• Their prices aren't the best for games.
• Alienated their Half-Life fan base in order to make a video game purely VR to try market their own VR equipment.
• If you have an opinion on the Steam forums, there's a chance an overzealous moderator doesn't agree with it and will delete your thread, give you warnings or ban you from the forums.
• For years they have done very little changes to the Steam client. It needs a new lick of paint.
• Disastrous and alleged rigging of the Steam F1 event.

My advice is to use GoG (good old games). I am not affiliated with them but, they seem to be a company that care more about their customers!

title: Steam does not follow Its own policies…
review:
Steam does not follow Its own policies and that they changed the prices of a game by stopping my purchase after I already put the game in the cart and hit the buy button.
I was making a purchase of Ark Survival Package when after I put it in the cart and entered my payment information. I received an error message “transaction would not go through” so I tried again and got the same message. On the third attempt with a different payment method I still received the same error but also saw a new error message that they were having technical difficulties try again later.
I waited 5-10 minutes and tried again but as I went to my cart the price had doubled. I immediately open a support ticket to let them know what happen and they admitted to technical difficulties but informed me, I had to pay the new price even though their policy states they will honor the sale price for 72 hours if you have trouble purchasing a game.
The policy states
“if your purchase fails, Steam will save your shopping cart (at the sale price) for three days (exactly 72 hours) so you can try to make your purchase again. Any discounts applied to your failed purchase will be saved, even if they are no longer available in the Steam store.

So if you're trying to buy a game that's on sale, but you have trouble purchasing, Steam will let you try the purchase again within the next three days.

We want you to be able to purchase and enjoy games on Steam. If your purchase fails, the three day period gives you time to sort out the issue with either Steam or your bank.”
I quoted their policy and explained that they have the information required to assist me and the final response given was?
Hi there,

I understand any possible frustration here but we have provided all the available information that we have regarding the matter.

With that being the case, I will now be closing this help request. If you do need help with an unrelated issue, please feel free to open a new help request and we will do our best to assist.

Steam Support
Kurt
Is there anything else I can do at this point?

review:

A common issue is I get the message "There have been too many login failures from your network in a short time period. Please wait and try again later."

Thing is, I get this message even after just starting up my PC for the first time in a couple of days, and it is the only PC I use to log in.
It's pretty frustrating. I have experienced other issues too, with messages claiming that my username or password is incorrect - when it definitely isn't.

I've tried several fixes I've found on forums that don't actually work. What usually works is just not using the service for a while and then trying again later - which is pretty shoddy, as there are paid-for products on there that I'm trying to use when my time allows, and the error is most definitely on Steam's end.

title: Good sales, Bad support, Some glitches, Bad 2FA
review:
Has good sales. App is well organized, but can be glitchy. Auto-run can be disabled (recommended). Supports playing with friends. Possibly the worst aspect of the design is that 2-factor authorization is only through a proprietary app, so you have to add a whole separate app to your phone in order to use app-based 2FA. A whole authorization app on your phone just for Steam.

Support from the store is useless. For example, Steam Controller on sale. I want two but can put only 1 in my cart so I write them a quick message to check if this is WAI. I watch for a reply. Day 3: controller is out of stock, so I update my ticket with a request for a rain check. Day 4: they finally reply with the message: controller out of stock; Only able to add 1 to cart at a time; Also this is technical support. Wow, they think I hadn't noticed it was out of stock? I reply with a second request for a rain check, and suggest they add a sales support contact to their website. Day 6: They reply saying the controller has no definite plans for production in the immediate future. They do not offer me any alternate promotions or give-aways as compensation for me missing out due to their very tardy reply, and due to their broken/poorly designed shopping cart which cannot ship two items to the same address at once (or their badly configured item pricing versus shipping, if they were using the shipping to subsidize the price and that's why they didn't want to ship 2 to the same address, which shouldn't cost twice as much).

Support tickets, I should add, can be made by choosing from a pre-set list of games and such very specific topics. There are not general topics, and barely any "other" type options. No "other" option is a beginning topic, sales questions don't relate to "I need sales (nontechnical) support making a purchase", and there's no "something else" option under "Purchases".

title: With a little extra love, Steam would be absolutely amazing!
review:
I have been using Steam for about 15 months now and have accumulated almost 100 games in that time. Take the bad reviews on here with a grain of salt as most of the complaints that I read seem to be from user error.

PROS:
1. Steam have a great "No questions asked" refund system that if you buy a game and don't like it, you can get the full refund as long as you haven't played the game for more than 2 hours. This has saved me a lot of money in the past and I can purchase any game in confidence knowing that if it doesn't hit my happy button, I can simply get a refund. The process for this is super quick too (as in under 20 mins), using their online tool directly through the platform.
2. The new interface as it is more visual and engaging.
3. I have a tower at home and a laptop for when I am on the go and I can stream through either device and the saves are automatic!
4. Wishlist the games that you like, Steam will always flick you a notification when they go on sale. It's a great way to save money and enjoy more of the games that you love.

CONS (mainly about the interface):
1. This is not really Steam's fault (although they could strike up a deal with the developers) but some games force you to sign up to another platform (like Origin for Star Wars Fallen Order) in order to play. While the game saves and appears in steam, every time I want to play that game, I have to open up Origin and play it through there. Once you accumulate enough games, you end up having too many different platforms open all at once just to play your favourite games. Steam should try to work with the developers so that you can open the game up directly through Steam. Steam could show the developers logo, or maybe ask the gamer for permission, or show a banner that says that the game is made by the developer each time you play the game, something to promote the developer but allowing the game to launch through steam would be way better than opening multiple other programs just to play the games. I buy the games through Steam, so I should be able to play the games through Steam. I shouldn't have to open/download and sign up to other platforms to play it (that's like ordering food from MacDonalds but having to pick it up at Hungry Jacks).
2. Sometimes during searches in the store page, clicking on a game, checking it out, and then deciding to return back to the store page, pressing the back button takes me back to the beginning of the store page. This means that I have to scroll allllllll the way back down to where I was to continue shopping. This is tedious and annoying and it would be great for Steam to remember where I last was browsing and so when I go back to the store page, it takes me right back to where I was.
3. There is no option to open up a game on the store page in a new window. Very frustrating for people that like organisation. When I get onto the store page and start scrolling down, it would be great to highlight/select/open up all the games that catch my eye and then go through them all, one by one, to decide which ones I want to "Wishlist," which ones that I want to buy and which ones I don't want. Having to open one game at a time and then after my decision of that game, I have to return back to the store page, only to be reloaded and sent to the very top of the page. This means that I have to scroll alllll the way down again to where I was last in order to try and refind my place, and this is annoying and a waste of time!
4. Have more than 4 pictures per game on the store page, or maybe replace the images with actual gameplay. I am a visual person who likes to see what the gameplay looks like. Most of the time when I see a game that I like, I click on it and the first thing I am shown is the game trailer, understandably. Usually, most trailers don't show a lot of actual gameplay. This means that I end up leaving Steam and jumping onto YouTube to find some actual gameplay. This is time consuming, tedious and probably is a loss of sales on your end. Having instant gameplay appear when I hover my mouse over a potential game would definitely entice me to want to see more, which could lead to more sales for you.
5. You should come up with your own Humble Bundle style monthly subscription program where subscribers can pay a couple of bucks a month and get a few games as a bundle. I sign up to Humble Bundle for their monthly games and always end up loading them into steam anyway. Your marketing team should be way ahead of the game here dominating this space since all the games purchased through Humble, all get loaded onto your platform anyway. I would support it.
6. Your customer service is not great, the reviewers are clear about this. Perhaps a little extra training or if you were to pull off step number 5 above, you could use that money to invest into more customer service staff tuning these irritate reviews into positive ones. This and a few more ideas I have up my sleeve could see you dominating this space within the next 12-24 months, easily.

Naturally, these are suggestions from my personal opinion and so I don't expect miracles (as based on the negative reviews on here, you have enough #!@$% to deal with, if you are even dealing with them), so I won't hold my breath for your response but when you next decide to throw us an update, these changes would be a lovely addition. Think about it.

Thanks and keep up the pretty good 'but needs attention' work!

title: Support is horrible at best...
review:
I usually am a massive fan of Steam but their Support is a joke at best.

I won a key for a game and by accident I redeemed it on the wrong account, yes that was my bad but I figured I try the support. They at first seemed willing to help and told me they might be willing to transfer the key if I provide a photo of the key and the bill. I told them that I won the key at a raffle and so have no bill for it but provided a photo of the key that was send to me. Then they told me that they will need an original photo of the original key so I contracted the organiser of the raffle. They sadly had already deleted the original email (it was a promo key they were given by the devs for a raffle) so they couldn't provide me with a photo of it either. I then decided to screenshot that conversation and forward it to the steam support and then the joke started. Because the organiser of the raffle used my real name in his reply and the name I used for the second steam account was not my real name (I mean who names their steam account after themself...) the support agent then accused me of account trading because the name of the account was different, he then further claimed that there was no prove that I participated in the raffle (even though I gave him the original email I got after winning the raffle and provided screenshots of the conversation with the raffle organizier) and closed with that the screenshots, that he specificly asked for before, were not considered as prove by him anymore... All of this took more then a week by now where I couldn't play the game.

I followed every instruction, I gave them every information they wanted, I was patient, and so on and that is the end result?

I like the platform, but that support is a bad joke.

title: A Hacker's Paradise
review:
I suffered my first hack about one month ago. On this occasion the automated system actually got me back in to my account fairly easily because only my password had been changed.

A couple of weeks later and I am again locked out. This time, however, the Steam security system is left all but helpless because the email address has also been changed as well as the password.

I eventually established that I was the true owner of the account and regained access for at least five minutes before it was snatched away again.

If the feeble efforts of Steam Support ever get me back in for a few more minutes, I will be closing my account.

I reckon I've probably got my money's worth out of the games I bought. I suspect many others have not been so lucky.

Unless this games platform becomes a lot more robust, it is only a matter of time before it collapses under the weight of fraudulent users.

title: Simply the best
review: Simply the best. Been Using Steam for 10+ years. Never had a problem. Have gotten games returned, NO problem. My account got hacked, TWICE, -- no problem. Never got charged, the protocols kept me safe and had my account fully recovered in a matter of hours. Just simply the best in every aspect and only getting better. My brief reading of the 1 star reviews, seems about 1/3rd are people who had their accounts hacked and are simply blaming steam for it. The one thing I think steam could do better, is managing the predatory actions that comes from being number one. Steam manages adequately at responding to these threats and has some wonderfully powerful utility built in to help combat them. However, the problem with predators is they target the weakest, and these tools need to be properly utilized in order to work. I would like to see a more pro-active campaign to stifle some of the most egregious scammers on the platform. Also a stronger and clear explanation of it's safety protocols. title: Steam is choosing to censor users…
review:
Steam is choosing to censor user reviews to appease large corporations who do anti-consumer things. When corporations/publishers/developers participate in anti-consumer behavior, then one avenue users have to warn others of such anti-consumer behavior is to leave reviews.

When consumers leave reviews about anti-consumer behavior, this is seen by the corporations who sell their products on steam as unwanted review fluctuations. Steam labels them "off-topic" "review bombs".

The corporations who sell their products on the steam platform, have pressured steam into developing a special review system that detects unusual fluctuations in review scores due to companies doing scummy things, and hide the fluctuation.

It's so hard for these companies to not do anti-consumer things that it's easier to hide the evidence than change their business practices.

While there is an opt out, these companies can bank on the fact that the bulk of users will not know this censoring system is in place, or that there is the option to turn it off in the settings. Apathy will allow companies to get away with anti-consumer practices with reduced repercussions.

A major issue with this is that the definition of off-topic is not well defined, Valve can not be depended upon to be transparent, or communicative, and the whole system is open to abuse. Furthermore it opens the door to further censorship in future.

Valve is selling out to the corporations, and giving them increased ability to abuse steam customers, through taking away those customer's voices.

title: Still Lacking Good Customer Support in 2019
review:
I love using steam, but the customer support sours the experience. In my experience with GOG, Sony, or pretty much any other major gaming company’s support, I've always had courteous customer service members that were understanding of the issues I've had and assisted me in any way they could. I wish I could say the same for steam.

Recently steam "replied" to the only refund request I’ve made while using the platform, but whoever was in charge never actually listened to the issue I had. I’m not angry that I didn’t get a refund, if there were some indication that they read and understood my problem, I’d be fine. I never held a grudge against Sony for denying a refund request of mine because they personally and politely explained why they couldn’t do it.

This is why I have a problem with my recent experience with steam, they simply denied my request a few minutes later with a premade response saying I played a couple hours over their time limit. Absolutely no apparent effort or care from whatever customer service member was on duty at the time (if there was one). This was the only time I’ve had to use the refund feature in nearly a decade of using steam, and I somewhat hope I don't have to use it again after this. Even though Steam has tried to improve their customer service recently, It’s still pretty bad. There’s still zero comparison to Nintendo, Sony, or GOG in that regard.

I wish every type of consumable media (books, movies, music) had something like Steam.

title: So a few days ago I tried to purchase a…
review:
So a few days ago I tried to purchase a bundle before the fall sale ended, but ran into issues as I could not get the bundle to add to my cart it would only offer the purchase as a gift option.
I was trying to buy the counterstrike bundle but it would not add as I already have the free version of csgo in my library. At first I thought this was some sort of glitch on my mobile app so I restarted the app still nothing I attempted to figure out how to remove the free version as I thought maybe that was the issue but was unable to figure it out how to on the app.

I've had demos of other titles before or other free versions and have not needed to remove them in order to upgrade to the full game or purchase the full game so naturally being that these are valve products and steam is a valve owned company youd expect them to have it worked out so people don't run into these issues, and to ice the cake without removing the free version I can add csgo to my cart and proceed to check out without receiving the product already owned or in library notification on the cart screen. Yet when I try to add the bundle i can't because it claims csgo is in my library. I mean yes I could have just bought the game by itself as it would add to the cart but when csgo was on sale for $8.47 and the bundle of all 3 counterstrike games was on sale for $8.29 it seems kind of illogical to pay more for less.

All this said I sent in a ticket sharing my frustration and questioning how this issue was occurring well expressing my confusion that it will let me add the game but not the bundle, and after waiting a day and a half received responce that basically says sorry there's nothing we can do for you, more a less it's not in steams policy to offer customers anything for having a bad experience with there service followed by the instructions on how to remove the free game from my library and the recommendation that I put the game on my wishlist and wait and see if it goes on sale followed by a shameless plug for there weekly sales and daily specials.

Here's a company that makes billions in revenue annually from people like myself who regularly spend on games, and then as a customer you reach out in frustration over a bad experience caused by issues with their store and they respond with sorry for the inconvenience, its not our policy to offer items at discounts before or after sales, so if you want the game your going to have to buy it at full price or wait, but in the mean time be sure to check our specials section for more great deals.... Great policy tell an upset customer who spends money every week on products that the only help you can provide is the advise to look at the other products that can be bought... Never in my life have I had a support representative try to sell me products when I'm complaining about a problem I had with their service while trying to purchase a product.

I didn't request the game for free or at a discount for my trouble, but did expect that they would make some sort of gesture. I sent in the ticket questioning why I was experiencing the issue to begin with and why I can add the game to my cart but not the bundle this was never answered instead I received steams policy on offering discounts outside of sales and steams sale Schule like it's not advertised enough everytime I open steam weekly deals deal of the day.

None the less as this is drawing out a bit long I'll end off with this after receiving the response I was less than satisfied and responded again in dissatisfaction and will have to wait another day and a half to see if I get another response and weather or not it will yelid any different results, I will 're write or post a new review if steam manages to show some better customer service support but as it stands now steam don't care about individual customers they make more than enough to survive if they loose a few customers so they don't go out of the way and try to give you a reason to continue using their services if you've had a issue they shrug it off. In contrast I messaged amazon a week ago after receiving 2 different shipping estimates when I placed an order. I just wanted to know which one was correct so I'd be home when it arrived, they clearly explained how their shipping system works let me know which estimate was the correct one and went above and beyond in apologizing for my confusion and credited my account a free month of prime which was worth more than the item I had purchased to begin with. Some company's care about their customers others only care about money I'll let you decide what type of company steam is.

title: Steam Revoked Legitimate Game Key
review:
A steam key for the PC game Call of Cthulhu was placed on sale in the Humble Store, I purchased it, and the key was sent to me and redeemed. I put three hours into the game. Humble later claimed they priced it incorrectly, refunded the amount, and revoked the key. Steam removed it from my account. The sale price should be honored for those who purchased a copy while it was active.

The notice I received from Steam was a lie:

"A Steam Product code you activated has been removed from your account.
Nov 21 Call of Cthulhu®

Your recent Product code activation has been revoked due to a problem processing payment for this item. The games associated with this product code can no longer be played as a result. This could be due a problem with the payment methods you used to acquire this item from a third-party seller; or a problem with how the third-party seller acquired the Product code. Please contact the seller you acquired the Product code from for assistance.

If you would like to purchase the item, please visit the Steam Store. Otherwise you may choose to delete the local content associated with these games."

The key was legitimate and purchased from a "legitimate" (if apparently not trustworthy) retailer, the transaction was completed and I have an electronic paper trail to prove it, the retailer screwed up and revoked the key. Whether this was driven by Steam, Humble Bundle, or the publishers, the practice was wholly unacceptable and a breach of consumer trust. Changing the price is fine - that's their right, but revoking transactions already completed under the sale price should not have been done.

title: Platform is nice, support is garbage
review:
Short review (long review in near future):

Great platform for buying/managing your games. You have everything at one place, which is really nice. Also, being able to share it with others is great (even though I'm the one sharing and all other have no games xD).

What's not so great, is the support.
If you ask for a refund, it doesn't matter that the reason is or how long you played it. They look at all their pre-given parameters (like "how long you already own this game?" and stuff like that) if only one is on the edge, you will not receive a refund.
Doesn't matter if you played the game for literally 10 minutes or 1 hour.
Nor does it make a difference if you already bought 300+ games on their platform and never refunded anything.
They should be more flexible according to this and offer a refund for a game if you don't even have 1 hour in the game and never refunded anything.
For other support-related questions, it's really a gamble. Sometimes they keep throwing links in your face so that they do not have to solve it themselves. Sometimes they actually help you. I guess it depends on who's working atm.

One minor additional con is that they do not have GTA 2 in the store anymore. Maybe it's a licensing problem, that's why I didn't take it into consideration upon rating.

More advanced version of this review TBA.

title: Pros & Cons
review:
I am troubled about how many stars i should give so i put the average. However i must note that i am an active player and will continoue using the platform.

Pros
- Endless game database.
- Steam sales resulting in extremely low prices.
- Steam keys being sold from 3rd party websites as bundles or single titles so you can get even better value for money.
- Steam keys can be gifted to friends directly.
- Game reviews from actual players.
- Social features incl. friend list & chat, news, groups, marketplace. I had some issues finding friends using common nicknames, but unfortunately steam will not let you use their account name.
- Direct player invite for multiplayer/coop games etc. without depending on the actual game's multiplayer interface. For example when playing online games that require certain ports that your firewall or router has closed for any reason, you can result in losing hours trying to connect through the game client, while through steam overlay you can connect directly.
- Workshop for finding user or official made content.

Cons
- Customer support; as you will notice from most reviews it can vary from extremely terrible to very helpful. I think the reason for this is purely a matter of people working in customer support. I noticed that the same issue not handled well or even rejected from one person was resolved from another after i insisted. So my advise is never give up even if you are totally frustrated from the replies you receive.
- Not all games are published through steam (e.g. new EA games are only found in their platform).
- Many old games are not well ported in steam and various new games have serious bugs, but developer support stops early. I strongly recommend reading the user comments before buying anything at all. You should realise that other users' opinions or issues may not be the case with you and should always check the overall picture instead of isolated good or bad reviews.
- Market monopoly is never good for developers or players.

title: I've dealt with this company since 2005 …
review:
I've dealt with this company since 2005 and my experience has been good overall but certainly not without its problems. I will start with the positive aspects. The products are priced fairly and the games library is almost always available with very little downtime. Support, while known for working at a snail's pace, has always been helpful and fair.

The negatives are a tad more nit-picky but have affected how I feel about the company more than anything else. The volunteers they task with managing the community (a/k/a the "community moderators") are absolute power hungry clowns who have caused many friends of mine to withdraw from participating in the community and even caused some to ditch Steam entirely in favor of alternatives. So long as this is allowed to continue I can only foresee more people becoming disenfranchised and wanting to leave.

Hopefully the company continues to focus on its strengths and gives their weaknesses the attention they require. Steam is a good thing overall but it truly feels like they have stopped caring as of late.

title: Problematic
review:
Simply does not follow EU legislation:

-selling adult games to minors without authority-based background-check
-binding minors to complex legal agreements, way above their heads, in violation of laws dictatating required accept from legal guardian
-mixing minors/adults in anonymous, unshielded online environments
-no 14-day EU-return-of-purchase for on-Internet purchase
-EU-region-price-differentiation & region-bound account - in clear violation with EU free moveability of goods
-customer support is... no support at all in clear violation of consumer responsabilty
-illegal invoices with no invoice number, company address, registration details and contact info as required according to law for registered companies
-closing accounts with payed-for-services, saying "you can always create a free new one" in disregard of invested value bound to them
-the fact you cant move a licence between accounts means you basically dont 'own' anything, not even a time-limted license. Steam can cancel anytime - you loose all. This business model violates multiple international laws
-Steam is openly selling faulty products, such as early access games, games out of support (abandoned by developers), etc and does not honor product reponsability
-VAC ban is some secret process, where Steam feel no obligation to explain cancellation of a legally purchased product without refund

If you look at it from a legal side, there really is a number of issues.

Steam by EU law has OBLIGATION to check age limit complienace. Like when when kids buy cigarettes or alcohol in shops. Shops cant just lean back and say 'customer told so'. But Steam does not follow law to check.

The problem is that EU-governments don't care & cant handle this kind of companies. Even though a lot of people think & feel everything is fine, it doesn't mean many also get hurt in the process.

Sad!

title: Unable to reset password
review:
I recently bought an HTC Vive VR headset and was obliged to register for a Steam account to install SteamVR, and enable me to use the headset. I was staying with my in-laws abroad at the time, but went right ahead and registered anyway, such that the in-laws could have a play with it before I returned to the UK. This included buying a game to try.
Moving on about 3 weeks and I'm back in the UK, returning from a New Year work trip and I've forgotten my password. With every other site in the world, there's a simple process where one can click on 'Forgotten my password' and have a new password emailed out. Apparently, this is a limited feature with Steam. Numerous attempts and no reminder email. So, onto contacting support to do this manually: support apparently cannot be contacted without logging in. Next, onto account recovery: this apparently also uses a verification email. Surprise, surprise, no sign of this email either!
So, I've a Steam account that I cannot log into, with purchases I cannot use, that I am unable to reset or refresh. In addition, I am now unable to use my VR headset even for free programs. If over-zealous security must be applied to a service, then at the very least, a system needs to be in place to deal with the exceptions. This is sadly not the case with Steampowered which gives me no choice than to rate the whole service as unacceptable.

title: Great Deals, Grab what you want on Sales
review:
Actually, this site is perfect, if you have the patience to wait until your game gets on sales. Use Wishlist and browse it often, or let it send you an e-mail notifying you about everything.
We live in a world now that even 5 year-old games are looking astounding. You can get anything you want, on any platform, including aside from Windows, Mac and Linux, too, for sometimes a price of 3 movie tickets.
And for that money, you will get much more entertainment than 6 hours and you can quit anytime and continue where you left off, even if the machine fails and you have to install everything again. In that case, you have a Steam Cloud for 85-90% of the games presented which will restore everything that you played before.
Speaking of Security, if you have Steam Mobile Authenticator and have your recovery code, then there is no way a hacker can get your password A N D your SMA at the same time! So feel free to trust it. If you lose your phone or it breaks, recover with the code you get from them, assemble on a new phone, and continue.
Steam Market is consistent and the users keep it alive. Spammers have to spend 5 dollars at least to try to spam and they have to set up their mobiles as well, which is only appealing for the dumbest of them.
You don't have to waste precious space in your home for digital copies, all are on the miraculous servers, that serve you at top-speed to get the games you have, anywhere, anytime.
I had some problems on Linux, but I'm still giving 5 stars, because Windows games are working perfectly, with minor glitches sometimes, but that can be said about all other companies. And on Linux was it fun as well for one-and-a-half year. The game companies rendered it bad, not Steam! They didn't care about Linux users. Not that Steam has cared perfectly, but did more support for the platform, than the game developers did.
So I quit on Linux because of the gaming companies, Steam did everything right, they provided the best experience. Shame that gaming companies don't need Linux customers.
You get bot-support, but that's not an issue, because if you stay within the "2-hour-in-2-weeks" policy, you'll get your money back, if something goes absolutely wrong.
Just watch for the machine specs and prepare to get the most for your money, especially twice a year. You know what I mean.
All-in-all, you will get your money's worth, maybe a lot more, just time it right. They have so many games, that if your wallet burns your pocket, you can spend anytime for a good game. Or wait until the "right time". Find out, if you don't believe me.

The Steam community is AWESOME, games are rated by people who state their honest opinion without reservation. Oftentimes they also share useful advice on how to make an old game work on your new Win platform. People actually take the time to write a useful review that helps others, so never skip the user review section before buying.

If you don't like the game you've purchased, you can also return it within 14 days if you hadn't played it more than 2 hours! I think that's a great return policy, that enables you to try a game and if you don't like it, you can just return it.

title: Fantastic prices, no service
review:
I've been an avid user of steam, and part of the community since September 2005.

There are constant sales and the prices are more than fair - and seeing as it's all saved in your Steam cloud / library there is no way you'll ever lose them unless you lose your account details - this is where the problem stems.

There is absolutely 0 customer service, or retention of any kind. They are trying to make everything completely automated and it doesn't work. If you lose your account, or your account gets hacked / scammed, it can take over 3 weeks for them to get back to you, sometimes they just say there's nothing they can do for you, or they want further proof you own the account - only to then have to wait another 3 weeks.

They don't do much for their community as such anymore either, all of the add-ons or DLC's such as new operations for CS:GO are all community made, yet they still make the pretty penny off it.

A fantastic application, instant access to purchased games, a good range of free-to-play games, but absolutely no customer service.

title: Great games, ok prices, terrible customer service
review:
Steam is the giant of gaming these days. If your game isn't made by EA and isn't on Steam, chances are it will sink incredibly quickly. There are millions of users on Steam at any given time, and what Valve has accomplished with Steam has revolutionized gaming. So why am I giving it two stars? Because for every good thing Valve has done, there lies some mistake they've made that has made me feel not so good with their company and service. Be it the rocky launch of the platform in the early 2000's, the "paid mods" debacle, the lack of refunds for many years, or the constant delaying of their own games, Valve has made many mistakes with their platform. Yet many people still sing praise of their "Lord Gaben" and spend thousands of dollars on games and hundreds more on in-game items. A knife in Counter-Strike: Global Offensive, for example, can fetch in the range of $400 USD. A knife. Your last ditch weapon in the game, one that you only pull out when you're out of ammo in both your primary and secondary weapons and cannot reload. You also pull it out when you want to run faster. And what does this $400 knife do? It turns your knife pearlescent pink and orange and shows how many kills you have with it.

Aside from creating a market for overpriced in-game items, Valve's support is notoriously bad. Months can go by before you receive a response, and even then it will most likely be a generic copy paste answer that does nothing. Other times, should you be locked out of your account, you might need to provide the CD key for the first game you registered to Steam. For some people, this is a code from a decade-and-a-half old game. And if they don't have this code? Their account, with potentially thousands of dollars worth of purchases behind it, remains sealed shut.

Valve really needs to rethink their approach with their products. The easygoing approach they have to their products, while it produces greatness, has lead to numerous delays, including their most popular series (one that also put them on the map) being delayed for over a decade with very little word on what's happening. Please, Valve. Get your act together.

title: Previously five stars, but the storefront is falling from grace
review:
Steam's a client that years ago entirely replaced my xbox in terms of usage. Now? It's still got excellent games, but there's way too many flaws to ignore.

1) An oversaturation of shovelware content. This ranges from bad indie games to downright cashgrabs that, had they bothered to check for half a second, wouldn't have possibly made it through their commonly exploited Greenlight system.

2) Support? What support? Their customer "support" happens to be the worst I've seen in a company. They'll leave your message alone for as long as two months or as short as a week, then they respond by giving you an automated message that has NOTHING to do with your question or problem.

3) No refunds. That's a real problem when combined with point 1.

4) Not for retro games. Valve won't test the games beforehand and make sure the publishers fixed it for modern Windows platforms, as proven in point 1. The prime example would be SW: Dark Forces II, which is in general a broken game. I'm talking color issues without 3D accel, lag issues with 3D accel, no soundtrack, and no fullscreen for cutscenes and menus. Again, no refunds... even if the product is genuinely broken.

Now that you've heard my issues with the client, let me just say that the client itself is excellent for games. It works well and doesn't get in the way often, and the amount of pro-consumer features such as Family Sharing make this platform look better than DRM-free gaming. However, it's time people stop ignoring that the store is being torn to shreds and the client is becoming increasingly hard to shop on.

Should these issues be fixed, I'll definitely update this review.

title: The only place you'll ever need to go for Digital Downloads.
review:
I initially registered for Steam in 2006, and have been using it ever since. There are so many facets of the system, but I'll address each individually. To summarize, it's been a grand adventure using their service, and I expect to see it running for the next hundred years.

First off, the store. The interface has changed over the years, but at present they've managed the tip-top of usability combined with reliability. The store as a whole has had some issues over the years, but they've always been resolved quickly. Buying a game is as simple as point and click, and voila. You've got a new game or new piece of software to play.

The Community, initially it was a simple forum, but it's quickly managed to surpass the game developer's own websites in use. There are some negatives, but the overall experience it provides is one that will leave you satisfied.

The Customer Support, having had to use their support systems over several issues, I can say that while I may have had every problem there could be, they have always been on the case to fix it and provide a rich and helpful amount of support.

I could sit down and write another 70,000 words on what Steam has done and still not cover everything. But all I can say in the end is that they created an entirely new marketspace, giving the developers, users, and everyday person something that simply didn't exist before. For those that haven't heard about it, it's a gateway to an endless source of games, software, and soon to be more than that.

Having spent more money than I'm happy to admit on this service, I don't regret it. Valve, and Steam will be here in a century without any doubt.

title: Absolutely Outstanding
review:
Steam is a great place to purchase computer games. Their selection of computer games is brilliant, and the only titles you won't find here are titles that greedy publishers like EA don't want to share. You'll find many AAA games as well as tons of indie games thanks to Steam Greenlight. You'll also find tons of user-created content thanks to the Steam Workshop.

Steam constantly has ridiculous sales as well, often going down to 75% on games during holidays such as Halloween and Christmas, as well as during Summer.

The Steam Client is also very, very robust. Although it suffers from the occasional performance issue, the program works well, and has a simple and clean UI. Download speeds in Hong Kong are quite acceptable, and you can change your download server settings in the Steam Client. Steam has quality servers all around the globe, and I once reached 13MB/s download speeds from the Shanghai server.

The social system in Steam is well designed. You can easily text and audio chat with other users, create chatrooms, communities, and groups, share status updates, screenshots, videos, game content and so on. There is also a good trading and market system, allowing you to trade games, game items and other miscellaneous items, and also purchase various user-sold items using.

There is more to say, but overall I've had a blast with Steam, and I've never had any issue whatsoever, so I can't comment on customer service.

title: The future of the gaming industry...
review:
Not only are their sales enticing instead of a monopoly, Steam has created a platform in the nature of Xbox Live as an overlay to their products: friend lists, instant messaging, clans, communities, modded content. Whilst this may be an irritation to certain users, I certainly enjoy it as a convenient addition - considerably surpassing older variants of a similar idea such as XFire - which isn't only smooth as in functional, it's productive to the experience.

Games are organised with your account, respectfully so without the software as intrusive copyright protection, to create a simplicity and the platform is regularly integrated into games for accessible modifications, removing the much tedious task of folder/file manipulation.

5 service. Never had a fault in performance, customer services are easy to contact if necessary and Steam is appropriate to my preference.

I just buy the games without discount when Im REALLY dieing for playing the game or there's too much time until discount season so well, I go ahead equally...

So much people complain about DRM Titles, yes, they have DRM Titles, OK...

You're advertised and you KNOW which ones are DRM, if you're a fool that installs the game in every computer you have then change your computer constantly well, it seems that you got enough money to buy the game again or you could just go ahead with the physical version that will not make you such a headaches...

That's out of the hands of Steam. I give Steam a 8.5/10 because of the Support for the people that don't know how to solve basic problems on the PC (Below the common gamers knowledge or just casuals)...

But for my Folks, the ones that knows what to do when we're in some trouble, you can go ahead without worrying about Steam CS and solve it yourself, giving steam a 9 or 9.5/ 10... (Because of the very closed and strict Game Genres divisions) (That's the only bad thing of it, so when I want a game I just go to the search bar type and enter and that's all)

Steam Rocks. DJKero.

title: Steam steals your money and has completely unacceptable support.
review: I bought Assassin's Creed III Deluxe Edition while it was on offer. Since I had a question regarding the offers I was in contact with the support(Takes 2-3 weeks for them to answer and one of the mails was written in a different language). For some reason the person suddenly refunded the game and put the money into my steam wallet without asking or anything. The person even claimed it was a special service(Even though according to the law you can get anything refunded with money back within 2 weeks, which this case was). I asked to get my game back but they told me I had to buy it again and now I have to pay full price. It is not just bad support but even legal issues.

title: Great Service
review:
Great service, been using it since it launched and have 100 of games through it. Can't remember the service ever being down and download rates are always great.

Negative reviews will come from people who have had a single game that doesn't work on their PC and then find getting support from the 3rd party hasn't worked. If this is your situation I suggest you ask Steam for a refund to your Steam Wallet (a store credit basically) as I think the customer service agents have more free-range to do this than refund to your bank account.

title: Not always the cheapest but gaurenteed to work
review: Never had any issues with Steam whatsoever. The summer sale and winter sales are the ones to watch as there's some bargains to be had.

title: Perfect way to buy and store your games!
review: Great! Just great! It's a cheap solution to store all of your games, and you can acces them from everywhere! I myself have 130 games, and i can download and continue to play everywhere!

title: Easy to use, catchy deals, support only so so.
review:
Steam is really easy to use and on top of that if you wait for the correct moment you can get cheap and catchy deals too.
My account is of huge value. Stories on the net of innocent people getting competely banned of Steam and never getting their money back do scare me.

title: Staggeringly good! Just a few little niggles...
review:
Steam as a platform is something I would find hard to do without in terms of gaming. It's so simple and intuitive to use, you can find the games you want with ease and they often have some "mad-to-miss" deals on the go (much to my wallet's displeasure)! I've had one or two issues with hacked accounts but they've always been dealt with in a swift manner.

The chat function is one of my least favourite parts, however, it seems that I do tend to get booted off a bit too often for my liking and occasionally the network does go down without any real reason sometimes.

I give it a 4 Star rating because of those small issues stated above, other than that, I really cannot fault it. Good work Valve!

title: NICE
review: Thumbs up for cs 1,6

title: I LOVE it
review:
The best service for gamers I ever found on the web! It's just really epic, the sales they hold are dirt cheap and you can talk with friend while playing games(even if they're not bought in Steam)!
I do mind the downtime that happens sometimes, but usually it's scheduled, so I won't argue with that

title: Total lack of concern for customers
review: I truly don't understand the positive rating. Customer service is a joke; they outright fail to keep promises, they offer no support, they don't even try. They trapped me into releasing a dispute through Paypal, and now they are ignoring me. To call what they offer customer support is a joke. Games don't work; they won't work with the game vendor (say so on the vendor site). The offer solutions that are meaningless or worse. Totally a disreputable company.

title: Best company in the world!
review: Enough said.

title: Good but could be better
review: I like the steam experience, it really did improve PC gaming and somewhat mad it close to console like communities like live and psn. Furthermore it also allow us to get good games cause it enables developers to sell their games in one place, and for us to buy them. On the con side there are games that are not well optimised and ported from console, and while it's developers fault, it would be good if steam had a selection of those games before putting them on store, something like apple's selection of apps. Also games are more than occasionally overpriced, and while steam says that it's developers price, they could also put a selection of those games, and tell the devs that it's just too much. I mean most of the time i just purchase cd-keys which are 50-70 % cheaper than steam price, not to mention retail versions of games that are cheaper as well. And if you take a look at steam so called deals you can see that some of the hit titles drop from 50- 30 euros for couple of days, which makes me wonder what's the real price of those games, and that it's just a joke. Other than that good community and online experience.

title: Awesome!!
review: I've been using steam for some years now and have the majority of my games bought through them. Reasonable download speeds and the fact that you can access your games from any PC make this a fantastic service to use, add to that the ridicculously cheap sales they have on a regular basis and I really can't praise them enough!!

title: Great service, works perfect, good servers
review:
I bought L.A. Noire on Steam at a sale for just 12,49€. Had some problems with the main menu not showing up on the screen. Wrote to them, their suggestions fixed it, even though I had searched all over the internet for answers, only theirs worked perfectly. :)

Also, the steam client itself and the friend stuff just works perfect, Thumps up for that! (Y)

They have great servers for the downloading as well. I use the Hamburg server, and I download with up to 1,1 MB/ps which is actually over the limit my connection is. (10/2)

title: Mixed pricing, slow d/l speeds
review:
I have used steam for a while now. I have bought a few games on it. I have a fast internet connection but the download speed (considering you are spending money on the games you're downloading!) is slow no matter what mirror you d/l from.
An investment in this area by the company would make downloading games a little more appealing.
Some of the games are cheap but some are more expensive than their shop counterparts. Perhaps I'm old school but to pay MORE for an item where you don't get a box, disc, manual or something solid in your hand is daylight robbery. Especially as they don't have to pay manufacturing costs, distribution costs etc. All downloads should be significantly cheaper.
Having said that I do like the fact they have 'events' when items are on sale...

title: The greatest digital distribution ever!
review: Steam has been around for years and is easily and obviously the number one digital distribution platform available for PC. Its a great way to get tons of games that are tied to your account, so you can play them anywhere. Huge library of games, both triple A and indie titles, a great friends list feature for super easy game-joining and chat, and the often and generous SALES on said titles make Steam the best place to go for PC gaming.

title: Good company with good games and community
review: really good games and sales

title: Steam ødelagde CS
review:
:(
Men god pålidelig shop.

RIP. COUNTER STRIKE

title: The gold standard
review:
For online game distrubution,Steam is the one everyone else wants to be.Title selection is top notch.Delivery meathod is simple.Pre-order bonus items are the norm.The sales are simply amazing.

You will pay a little bit more for the typical game here,but a paitent gamer can find unbeatable deals.

title: sacred 2 and about 8 other games
review:
When steam first came around, I wanted nothing to do with it. But after a few years, steam is the ONLY place worth my time and money. There download speeds are always TOP!.

I have had to use there customer service, but from what my 2 bros have to say, "There fast, courteous, and value your time". Thats allot to say now-a-days. Especially since theres a few digital download sites out there that blatantly respond out of tickets saying your wasting there time.

I just want to personal Thank steam for offering there services. 13 games and counting.

title: Great service and full of sales
review:
If you would have said to me 2 yrs ago to use steam I would have said no, but today it’s the only place I go for games.
The game downloads are fast and safe. There are brilliant sales so you can pick up top games for a few pounds.
On the down side some of the new games don't always work but the support forums are really useful and you can fix any problem that comes up within seconds.
One of the best feature is the pre-launch downloads were you can download a game like dragon age2 before its released and its ready to play once the clock hits midnight on the release date very cool !!

title: Where is the customer service?
review:
I have used steam since 2005 and thought it was such a great innovative idea.
Unfortunately it was pretty buggy and clunky to use.
Fast forward to 2011 and Steam has really improved and many game publishers are hooking up with Steam.
Unfortunately customer service has left something to be desired.
I recently purchased Magicka from Steampower, but unfortunately Magicka's multiplayer seems to be completely broken. To this day I cannot play a multiplayer game for longer than a hour due to the ridiculous amount of bugs. Most multiplayer games end in seconds or minutes due to bugs.

Steampowered's customer service refuses to offer any compensation for the broken game I have purchased. No refunds, partial refunds, credits, etc. Just a straight up restatement of no refunds over and over.
I have also allowed reasonable time for the game developers to address the issue.

With poor customer service I cannot give anything higher than 3/5 stars.

title: Good overall - Slight drawbacks
review:
I have owned a Steam account now for 7 years and bought around 20 games from it now. It is generally very fast, but is occasionally bogged down by new releases.
The new and improved Steam Community has been a bit of an issue lately, causing problems with Punkbuster (showing it up as a cheat - but this is Punkbusters problem, not Steam's). As well as this, it has also been very slow and has been very buggy along with a countless amount of maintenance peroids.

I prefered the old 'WON' system run by Valve 'back-in-the-day' but needless to say, Steam is a sound system but needs them few adjustments.

title: My Favourite Game Store
review:
Background: I own steam account for 7 years. I currently have 99 games on it. I had problems few times (steam store could not be displayed, I forgot my password as I had autologin and I had no reason to reinstall OS).
My issues were solved promptly. Each within 2 hours by support.

I can say that I was bit disapointed with early version of steam client but that is past long gone and now steam performance is top of this kind of application. I miss only possibility to join multiplayer games where I have my friends currently playing (with Battlefield 2 and some other games).

My ratting is: 1. 10/10 trust while shoping 2. 9/10 steam interface 3. 10/10 steam can distinct your country and will not sell you hame you can't play there 4. good support 5. 9/10 ability to join games with friends + steam overlay (may cause false positives with punkbuster). My overal rating is 9.5 of 10 points.
By this review I wish steam and us gamers many and many pappy years togather.

title: TF2 + L4D = best FPS ever
review: So much diversity in those two games. Lets not forget classics like CS which spawned the era of competitive gaming. The website itself is easy to navigate and very infomative for those who looking for deals and best sellers.

title: Great
review:
an easy to use website
multiple payment methods (paypal, ideal)
great discounts

prety expensive

title: Good intentions gone bad
review: I downloaded a program from Steam through their website. The download was smooth but the installation was glitchy. Ultimately the game would not work, so - I tried to contact Steam tech support. No tech support phone line, everything is done through email. My question involved a patch that has to be applied to the game, but the game had to be run on Steam servers, so what was I to do? I waited one week to finally hear back from Steam and they didn't even answer my question. It was two weeks before they got around to answering my question but still did not resolve my problem. I have played other games on Steam, but those are ones that I bought the disk and played through the interface and without a problem. My issue is that Steam essentially has no tech support and when you have a problem, you are on your own. I'm out over $35 dollars, no big deal but it would have been nice if Steam had at least pretended to back up the products that they sell. Oh - and they have a absolute NO REFUND policy, so don't even think about going there.. ever.

title: effective and clear. does nicely for its purpose
review:
A website connected to an application that does what it's made for. The websites is not always the fastest, but it always functions as expected. Orders by credit card work excellent. Games are downloaded with easy and playing is just as simple.

Compared to retail stores it is cheaper, but is pretty expensive when you realise the way they convert dollar to euro.
